from.title: Heart Of Flesh Ezekiel
from.body: # Heart of Flesh (Ezekiel) ## The oracle "I will give them one heart, and a new spirit I will put within them. I will remove the heart of stone from their flesh and give them a heart of flesh." ## Date / context Ezekiel among the exiles in Babylon, c. 590s BC. ## Fulfillment Paul claims the Corinthians are letters of Christ written by the Spirit "on tablets of human hearts" (2 Cor 3:3); fulfilled …

to.title: New Heart Ezekiel 36
to.body: # New Heart (Ezekiel 36) ## The oracle "I will sprinkle clean water on you, and you shall be clean… A new heart I will give you, and a new spirit I will put within you… and I will put my Spirit within you, and cause you to walk in my statutes." ## Date / context Ezekiel's restoration oracle to the exiles. ## Fulfillment Jesus assumes it as common knowledge with Nicodemus ("born of water and Spirit…

from.title: Ezekiel 10 Glory Departs
from.body: # Glory Departs the Temple → Christ Departs the Temple ## OT Story — Ezekiel 10-11 The glory of the LORD lifts from the cherubim, moves to the threshold, and finally to the mountain east of the city. The temple is left desolate before destruction. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 23:38-24:2: 'Your house is left to you desolate'; not one stone left. - Luke 21:5-6: Foretelling the temple's fall. - Mark 1…

to.title: Ezekiel 43 Glory Returns
to.body: # Glory Returns from the East → Christ Returns; God Dwells With Us ## OT Story — Ezekiel 43:1-9 Ezekiel sees the glory of the LORD coming from the east — the same direction it had departed — and filling the new temple. 'I will dwell in their midst forever.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 2:1-4: Spirit fills the house with sound from heaven. - Revelation 21:3: God's dwelling is with man. - Matthew 24:27:…

from.title: Feast Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Feast Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Matt 22; Luke 14; Rev 19 From Passover to Sinai's covenant meal, to Isaiah 25's feast on the mountain, to the Last Supper, to the marriage supper — feasts are eschatological signs. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — Passover meal. - **Exod 24:9-11** — Covenant meal on Sinai. - **Isa 25:6** — Feast of fat things on this mountain. ## Shared structure - Shared…

to.title: Last Supper As Passover
to.body: # Last Supper as Passover ## NT Text — Matt 26:17-30; Mark 14:12-26; Luke 22:7-23 The Last Supper is celebrated as a Passover meal, with Jesus reinterpreting bread and cup as his body and blood. The Exodus typology comes to its fulfillment: the true Passover Lamb is at table. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — The Passover meal in Egypt. - **Exod 24:8** — Blood of the covenant sprinkled on the peop…

from.title: soter
from.body: # soter (σωτήρ) — savior ## Semantic field Savior, deliverer. Used in Greco-Roman world for emperors, gods, and benefactors; the NT applies it to God and Christ, often polemically. ## Theological weight Jesus is "the Soter of the world" (John 4:42). Titus 2:13 famously: "our great God and Soter, Jesus Christ." The title contests imperial claims with christological reality. ## Septuagint mapping Re…

to.title: soteria
to.body: # soteria (σωτηρία) — salvation ## Semantic field The state or event of being saved — deliverance, preservation, salvation. Noun of sozo. ## Theological weight "The gospel… is the power of God for soteria to everyone who believes" (Rom 1:16). Soteria includes justification, sanctification, and glorification — past, present, future deliverance. ## Septuagint mapping Renders Hebrew yashua / yeshuah.…

from.title: Day Of Atonement
from.body: # Day of Atonement → Christ's Atoning Work The most explicit and thoroughly-developed OT-to-NT typology in scripture. The book of Hebrews (chapters 7-10) is essentially an extended commentary on this very pattern. If any typology has *canonical-strength* warrant, this is it. ## OT Story — Leviticus 16 Once a year, on the **10th day of the 7th month** (Tishri), Israel observes Yom Kippur: 1. The hi…

to.title: Scapegoat Azazel
to.body: # Scapegoat into the Wilderness → Sin Borne Away ## OT Story — Leviticus 16:20-22 The high priest lays both hands on the live goat, confesses Israel's sins over it, and a fit man leads it to a solitary place. The goat bears all iniquities away. ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:29: The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world. - Isaiah 53:6: The LORD has laid on him the iniquity of us all. - Hebre…

from.title: Glory Filling New Jerusalem
from.body: # Glory Filling the New Jerusalem ## The appearance The New Jerusalem has no temple, "for its temple is the Lord God the Almighty and the Lamb"; it needs no sun or moon, "for the glory of God gives it light, and its lamp is the Lamb." ## Mode / context Revelation 21-22, the consummation. ## Significance The shekhinah that filled tabernacle and temple now fills the entire city; God's presence is th…

to.title: Glory Filling Temple Solomon
to.body: # Glory Filling Solomon's Temple ## The appearance At the temple dedication, the cloud fills the house of YHWH so that the priests cannot stand to minister; fire descends and consumes the offerings, and the glory of YHWH fills the house. ## Mode / context Solomon's dedication, c. 957 BC; covenant-renewal liturgy. ## Significance The Sinai-tabernacle pattern reaches its architectural climax — YHWH …

from.title: Day Of Atonement High Priest
from.body: # High Priest on the Day of Atonement → Christ's Greater Entry ## OT Story — Leviticus 16 Once a year the high priest, after washing and offering for his own sin, enters the most holy place with blood, sprinkles the mercy seat, and emerges to bless the people. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 9:6-12: Christ entered once for all, with his own blood, into the greater tent not made with hands. - Hebrews 4…

to.title: Mercy Seat Atonement
to.body: # Mercy Seat / Hilasterion → Christ Our Propitiation ## OT Story — Exodus 25:17-22; Leviticus 16:14-15 Over the ark sits the kapporet, the mercy seat, flanked by two cherubim. Once a year the high priest sprinkles blood there for the sins of the people. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 3:25: God put forward Christ as a propitiation (hilasterion). - Hebrews 9:5: The cherubim of glory overshadowing the me…

from.title: Bride Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Bride Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Eph 5; Rev 19, 21; John 3:29 From Eve to the New Jerusalem, the bride is a pattern of covenant love. Israel as bride, faithless and beloved; the church as bride; the city as bride. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:21-24** — Eve formed from Adam's side. - **Hos 1-3** — YHWH's bride. - **Isa 62:5** — Bridegroom rejoices over bride. - **Ezek 16** — Faithless bride…

to.title: Ezekiel 16 Faithless Bride
to.body: # Ezekiel 16 / Faithless Bride → Cleansed and Clothed ## OT Story — Ezekiel 16 Jerusalem is depicted as a newborn unwanted girl, washed and clothed by the LORD, made a queen. She plays the harlot. Yet he will atone and re-establish the covenant. ## NT Fulfillment - Ephesians 5:25-27: Christ gave himself to cleanse her by washing. - Revelation 19:7-8: The bride has made herself ready, granted fine …

from.title: Tongues Of Fire
from.body: # Tongues of Fire ## NT Text — Acts 2:3-4 Divided tongues 'as of fire' rest on each disciple. The Sinai-fire that descended on the mountain now rests on the people, individualizing the Shekinah. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19:18** — Sinai descended in fire. - **Exod 3:2** — Burning bush — fire that does not consume. - **Mal 3:2** — Like a refiner's fire. ## Shared structure - Divine fire - Resting …

to.title: Pentecost Tongues
to.body: # Pentecost Tongues ## What happens Fifty days after Passover, the Spirit descends on the gathered disciples with sound of wind and tongues of fire; they proclaim God's mighty works in the many languages of the nations gathered in Jerusalem. ## Typological significance Babel reversed — scattered tongues regathered around one gospel. Sinai's fire on the mountain becomes Spirit-fire on each believer…

from.title: Psalm 69 Reproaches Fell On Me
from.body: # Psalm 69 / 'Zeal for Your House Has Consumed Me' → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Psalm 69 The psalmist drowns in deep waters; reproach has broken his heart; they gave him vinegar to drink; zeal for God's house consumes him. ## NT Fulfillment - John 2:17: 'Zeal for your house will consume me' at the temple cleansing. - John 15:25: 'They hated me without cause.' - Romans 15:3: The reproaches of t…

to.title: Words From Cross I Thirst
to.body: # Word from Cross: I Thirst ## NT Text — John 19:28 Jesus says 'I thirst' to fulfill Scripture. The Bread-of-Life is consumed by thirst that he might quench ours. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 69:21** — Vinegar for my thirst. - **Ps 22:15** — Tongue cleaves. - **Isa 53:10** — Soul makes offering for sin. ## Shared structure - Thirst expressed - Sour wine offered - Fulfillment formula - Reversal ## Theo…

from.title: Romans 11 Olive Tree Grafted
from.body: # Romans 11: Olive Tree Grafted ## NT Text — Rom 11:17-24 Paul portrays Israel as an olive tree whose natural branches were broken off and wild Gentile branches grafted in. The hope is restoration of all Israel. ## OT Antecedents - **Jer 11:16** — Green olive tree, fair with goodly fruit. - **Isa 17:6** — Gleanings of olive harvest. - **Hos 14:6** — Beauty like the olive. ## Shared structure - Oli…

to.title: Vine Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Vine Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 15 Israel as vine in Psalm 80 and Isaiah 5 — disappointing fruit. Christ as the True Vine, with believers as branches abiding in him. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 80:8-16** — Vine brought out of Egypt. - **Isa 5:1-7** — Vineyard of the LORD. - **Jer 2:21** — Choice vine turned degenerate. - **Hos 10:1** — Israel a luxuriant vine. ## Shared structure - Plan…

from.title: 1 Cor 5 Christ Our Passover
from.body: # 1 Cor 5: Christ Our Passover ## NT Text — 1 Cor 5:7-8 Paul calls Christ 'our Passover lamb, sacrificed for us,' and exhorts cleansing of old leaven. Passover and Unleavened Bread become ethical patterns. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — Passover ordinance. - **Exod 13:6-7** — Unleavened bread for seven days. - **Deut 16:1-8** — Passover-Unleavened Bread combined. ## Shared structure - Lamb slai…

to.title: Last Supper As Passover
to.body: # Last Supper as Passover ## NT Text — Matt 26:17-30; Mark 14:12-26; Luke 22:7-23 The Last Supper is celebrated as a Passover meal, with Jesus reinterpreting bread and cup as his body and blood. The Exodus typology comes to its fulfillment: the true Passover Lamb is at table. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — The Passover meal in Egypt. - **Exod 24:8** — Blood of the covenant sprinkled on the peop…

from.title: Obadiah Day Of The LORD
from.body: # Obadiah / Day of the LORD on Edom → Final Judgment ## OT Story — Obadiah 1:15-21 'The day of the LORD is near upon all the nations… As you have done, it shall be done to you.' The kingdom will be the LORD's. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 12:19: Vengeance is mine, says the Lord. - Revelation 19:11-21: The rider judges the nations. - Hebrews 12:23: Mount Zion's assembly. ## Shared structure - Justice…

to.title: Zechariah 14 Day Of The LORD
to.body: # Day of the LORD / Feet on the Mount of Olives → Christ Returns ## OT Story — Zechariah 14:1-9 'On that day his feet shall stand on the Mount of Olives, which is east of Jerusalem, and the mount shall be split in two… The LORD will be king over all the earth. On that day the LORD will be one and his name one.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 1:11-12: Angels say Jesus will return as he went; the place wa…

from.title: Psalm 2 Anointed King
from.body: # Psalm 2 / 'You Are My Son' → Messiah Enthroned Despite Nations' Rage ## OT Story — Psalm 2 Nations rage and kings plot against the LORD and his anointed. The LORD laughs; he installs his King on Zion. 'You are my Son; today I have begotten you. Ask of me, and I will give the nations as your inheritance.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 4:25-28: Quoted as fulfilled in the plot against Jesus. - Acts 13:3…

to.title: Psalm 8 Son Of Man
to.body: # Psalm 8 / Son of Man Crowned → Christ's Dominion Restored ## OT Story — Psalm 8 'What is man that you are mindful of him, the son of man that you care for him?' Made a little lower than the heavenly beings, crowned with glory, all things under his feet. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 2:5-9: Applied to Jesus — we see Jesus crowned with glory and honor. - 1 Corinthians 15:27: All things put in subjec…

from.title: Abraham Isaac
from.body: # Abraham / Isaac → The Crucifixion ## OT Story — Genesis 22 God commands Abraham: *"Take your son, your only son, whom you love — Isaac — and go to the region of Moriah. Sacrifice him there as a burnt offering on a mountain I will show you."* (Gen 22:2) Abraham obeys. Isaac carries the wood for his own sacrifice up the mountain. Isaac asks, *"Father, where is the lamb?"* Abraham answers, *"God hi…

to.title: Joseph Sold By Brothers
to.body: # Joseph Sold by His Brothers → The Betrayal and Vindication of Christ ## OT Story — Genesis 37, 40, 41-50 Joseph is the **beloved son** of Jacob, given a special garment (the "coat of many colors"). He has prophetic dreams in which his brothers and parents bow down to him. His brothers hate him and plot to kill him; instead, on Judah's suggestion, they sell him for **twenty pieces of silver** to …

from.title: City Throughout Scripture
from.body: # City Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Heb 11:10; Rev 21 From Cain's first city to Babel, Sodom, Jerusalem, Babylon, and the New Jerusalem, cities embody human collective destiny. Babylon vs. Zion is the canon's tale of two cities. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 4:17** — Cain founds a city. - **Gen 11** — Babel. - **Ps 48** — Beautiful Zion. - **Isa 47** — Babylon doomed. ## Shared structure - Collec…

to.title: Garden Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Garden Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 18-20; Rev 22 Eden, Song of Songs' enclosed garden, Gethsemane, the resurrection garden, the city-garden of Revelation 22 — gardens trace the arc of communion lost, recovered, consummated. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2-3** — Eden garden. - **Song 4:12-16** — Sealed garden of love. - **Isa 51:3** — Wilderness made like Eden. - **Ezek 36:35** — Land beco…

from.title: Haggai Glory Of Latter House
from.body: # Glory of the Latter House → Christ Enters the Second Temple ## OT Story — Haggai 2:6-9 'Yet once more I will shake the heavens and the earth… and the desire of all nations shall come, and I will fill this house with glory… The glory of this latter house shall be greater than the former.' ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 12:26-29: Cited as future shaking, leaving only the unshakable. - Luke 21:5-6: Th…

to.title: Temple Dedication Glory
to.body: # Glory Fills the Temple → Glory Fills the Church ## OT Story — 1 Kings 8:10-13 When the priests bring the ark into the new temple, the cloud fills the house, so the priests cannot stand. The LORD has filled his house with glory. ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:14: The Word became flesh and tabernacled, full of glory. - Acts 2:1-4: The Spirit fills the house at Pentecost. - Revelation 21:22: The Lord G…

from.title: Revelation 4 Throne Vision
from.body: # Revelation 4: Throne Vision ## NT Text — Rev 4 John sees the throne with the rainbow, four living creatures, twenty-four elders — gathering Ezekiel 1, Isaiah 6, and Daniel 7 into one vision. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 1** — Four living creatures around the throne. - **Isa 6** — Seraphim and Holy, Holy, Holy. - **Dan 7:9-10** — Ancient of Days enthroned. ## Shared structure - Heavenly throne - Su…

to.title: Revelation 6 Four Horsemen
to.body: # Revelation 6: Four Horsemen ## NT Text — Rev 6:1-8 Conquest, war, famine, death — four horsemen unleashed by the seals. Zechariah's coloured horses fund the imagery. ## OT Antecedents - **Zech 1:8-11** — Coloured horses patrolling earth. - **Zech 6:1-8** — Four chariots with horses. - **Ezek 14:21** — Four severe judgments: sword, famine, plague, beasts. ## Shared structure - Four horses with ri…

from.title: Bride Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Bride Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Eph 5; Rev 19, 21; John 3:29 From Eve to the New Jerusalem, the bride is a pattern of covenant love. Israel as bride, faithless and beloved; the church as bride; the city as bride. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:21-24** — Eve formed from Adam's side. - **Hos 1-3** — YHWH's bride. - **Isa 62:5** — Bridegroom rejoices over bride. - **Ezek 16** — Faithless bride…

to.title: Leah Rachel Two Brides
to.body: # Leah and Rachel / Two Brides → Israel and the Church Made One ## OT Story — Genesis 29:15-30 Jacob loves Rachel but is deceived into marrying Leah first. Both bear sons to him; together with the handmaids they produce the twelve tribes. ## NT Fulfillment - Ephesians 5:25-32: Christ loves one bride, the church. - Galatians 3:28: One people in Christ from Jew and Gentile. - Ruth 4:11: 'Rachel and …

from.title: Isaiah 42 First Servant Song
from.body: # First Servant Song → The Gentle Servant Bringing Justice ## OT Story — Isaiah 42:1-9 'Behold my servant, my chosen, in whom my soul delights. I have put my Spirit upon him; he will bring forth justice to the nations. A bruised reed he will not break.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 12:15-21: Matthew applies the entire passage to Jesus' ministry. - Matthew 3:17: 'This is my beloved Son' echoes 'my c…

to.title: Isaiah 50 Third Servant Song
to.body: # Third Servant Song / I Gave My Back → Christ Set His Face ## OT Story — Isaiah 50:4-9 'I gave my back to those who strike, my cheeks to those who pull out the beard… I have set my face like a flint; I know I shall not be put to shame.' ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 9:51: He set his face to go to Jerusalem. - Matthew 26:67: They spat in his face and struck him. - Matthew 27:14: He gave no answer. ## S…

from.title: Hezekiahs Sundial
from.body: # Hezekiah's Sundial → Time Reversed as Sign of Healing ## OT Story — 2 Kings 20:8-11 As a sign that Hezekiah will be healed, the shadow on the sundial moves backward ten steps. God interrupts the irreversible. ## NT Fulfillment - John 11:25: I am the resurrection. - 1 Corinthians 15:54-57: Death is swallowed up in victory. - Joshua 10:13: Sun standing still — analogous sign. ## Shared structure -…

to.title: Sun Stands Still
to.body: # Sun Stands Still for Joshua → Day That Never Ends ## OT Story — Joshua 10:12-14 At Joshua's prayer, the sun stands still over Gibeon and the moon over Aijalon. There was no day like it before or since — the LORD fought for Israel. ## NT Fulfillment - Revelation 22:5: No need of sun, for the Lord God is their light. - Habakkuk 3:11: Sun and moon stood still. - Isaiah 60:19-20: Your sun shall no m…

from.title: 1 John 2 Atoning Sacrifice
from.body: # 1 John 2: Atoning Sacrifice ## NT Text — 1 John 2:1-2 If anyone sins we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous. He is the propitiation for our sins — and for the whole world. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement propitiation. - **Isa 53:10** — Soul an offering for sin. - **Ps 32:1-2** — Blessed is he whose sin is covered. ## Shared structure - Advocate - Propit…

to.title: Hebrews 2 Tasted Death For Everyone
to.body: # Hebrews 2: Tasted Death for Everyone ## NT Text — Heb 2:9-18 Jesus, made lower than angels for a little while, tasted death for everyone. He is a merciful and faithful high priest because he shared in our flesh. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 8** — Made lower than the heavenly beings. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear iniquities. - **Job 19:25** — Redeemer who stands. ## Shared structure - Incarnation as solidar…

from.title: Bread Broken Body
from.body: # Bread Broken as Body Given ## NT Text — Luke 22:19; 1 Cor 11:23-24 Jesus breaks bread and identifies it with his body 'given for you.' The act gathers manna, Passover unleavened bread, and the showbread into a single self-offering. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 16** — Manna as bread from heaven. - **Exod 12:8** — Unleavened bread of Passover. - **Lev 24:5-9** — Twelve loaves of the showbread. ## Sh…

to.title: Feast Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Feast Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Matt 22; Luke 14; Rev 19 From Passover to Sinai's covenant meal, to Isaiah 25's feast on the mountain, to the Last Supper, to the marriage supper — feasts are eschatological signs. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — Passover meal. - **Exod 24:9-11** — Covenant meal on Sinai. - **Isa 25:6** — Feast of fat things on this mountain. ## Shared structure - Shared…

from.title: Psalm 45 Wedding Of King
from.body: # Psalm 45 / Royal Wedding → Marriage of the Lamb ## OT Story — Psalm 45 A royal wedding song. The king's throne is forever; his garments are fragrant; the bride is beautiful in her chambers. 'Forget your people; the king will desire your beauty.' ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 1:8-9: 'Your throne, O God' applied directly to the Son. - Ephesians 5:25-32: The mystery of marriage refers to Christ and t…

to.title: Psalm 72 Royal Reign
to.body: # Psalm 72 / Solomon's Reign → Messiah's Universal Just Reign ## OT Story — Psalm 72 A psalm of Solomon: he judges with righteousness, defends the poor, kings of Sheba bring gifts, all nations are blessed in him, his name endures forever. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 2:11: Magi bring gold, frankincense, myrrh — echo of Sheba. - Revelation 21:24-26: Kings bring glory into the city. - Galatians 3:14:…

from.title: Hebrews 1 Heir Of All Things
from.body: # Hebrews 1: Heir of All Things ## NT Text — Heb 1:1-4 God has spoken in these last days by his Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom also he made the world. Christ as final word and inheritor. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 2:8** — Ask of me, and I will give you the nations. - **Prov 8:22-31** — Wisdom present at creation. - **Deut 32:8-9** — Inheritance allotted. ## Shared structure …

to.title: Hebrews 2 Tasted Death For Everyone
to.body: # Hebrews 2: Tasted Death for Everyone ## NT Text — Heb 2:9-18 Jesus, made lower than angels for a little while, tasted death for everyone. He is a merciful and faithful high priest because he shared in our flesh. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 8** — Made lower than the heavenly beings. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear iniquities. - **Job 19:25** — Redeemer who stands. ## Shared structure - Incarnation as solidar…

from.title: Manna In Wilderness
from.body: # Manna in the Wilderness → Bread of Life ## OT Story — Exodus 16 God rains bread from heaven; Israel gathers a day's portion. None on the Sabbath. It must be eaten daily; hoarded manna spoils. ## NT Fulfillment - John 6:31-35: 'I am the bread of life' — Jesus contrasts manna and himself. - Revelation 2:17: To the one who conquers I will give hidden manna. - Deut 8:3 / Matt 4:4: Man lives by every…

to.title: Quail Provided
to.body: # Quail in the Wilderness → Warning Against Craving ## OT Story — Exodus 16:13; Numbers 11:31-34 God provides quail twice — first as gift alongside manna, later in judgment when Israel craves meat. The graves of craving (Kibroth Hattaavah) mark the second. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Corinthians 10:6: These things happened as examples that we might not desire evil. - Philippians 3:19: Their god is their…

from.title: Empty Tomb
from.body: # Empty Tomb ## NT Text — Matt 28:6; John 20:6-7 The tomb is empty; grave-clothes remain. The body is raised, not stolen. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 16:10** — You will not abandon my soul to Sheol. - **Hos 6:2** — Third day he will raise us. - **Isa 53:10-11** — He shall see his offspring. ## Shared structure - Tomb opened - Linens left - No body - Angelic announcement ## Theological motif Three-day…

to.title: Sealing Of Stone
to.body: # Sealing of the Stone ## NT Text — Matt 27:62-66 Pilate authorizes a guard and seal on the tomb. The precaution becomes evidence: theft cannot explain the empty tomb. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 6:17** — Sealed lions' den. - **Lam 3:53** — Stone over the pit. - **Job 14:13** — Hide me in Sheol. ## Shared structure - Stone over opening - Official seal - Guard posted - Confinement of righteous ## The…

from.title: Crossing Red Sea Baptism
from.body: # Crossing the Red Sea → Baptism Into Moses, Baptism Into Christ ## OT Story — Exodus 14 Israel walks through the sea on dry ground; the waters return and drown Pharaoh's army. The old life of slavery is buried in the sea. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Corinthians 10:1-2: 'All were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.' - Romans 6:3-4: Baptized into Christ's death, raised to walk in newness. - …

to.title: Water Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Water Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 4; John 7; Rev 22 Creation's waters, flood, Red Sea, wilderness rocks, Jordan, the Samaritan well, baptism, the river of life — water marks judgment and life. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1:2** — Spirit hovering over waters. - **Exod 14** — Red Sea passage. - **Ezek 47** — River from the temple. ## Shared structure - Chaos-water - Judgment-water - Delive…

from.title: Bride Of Christ Eschatological
from.body: # Bride of Christ (Eschatological) ## The oracle The marriage of the Lamb has come, and his Bride has made herself ready; the New Jerusalem comes down "as a bride adorned for her husband." ## Date / context Revelation's climactic visions; rooted in the OT marriage metaphor for YHWH and Israel. ## Fulfillment Awaits the consummation; the church already betrothed (2 Cor 11:2) lives toward the weddin…

to.title: New Jerusalem Descending
to.body: # New Jerusalem Descending ## The oracle John sees the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride; God dwells with humanity, every tear wiped away, no more death, mourning, or pain. ## Date / context Climax of Revelation; Ezekiel's temple vision is a major OT antecedent. ## Fulfillment Awaits consummation; foretasted in the church as the city set on a hill. #…

from.title: Hebrews 7 Forever Priest
from.body: # Hebrews 7: A Priest Forever ## NT Text — Heb 7:23-28 Christ holds his priesthood permanently because he continues forever. He is able to save to the uttermost because he always lives to intercede. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 110:4** — Priest forever. - **Lev 16** — Annual atonement — repeated. - **Num 25:13** — Phinehas's perpetual priesthood — partial type. ## Shared structure - Eternal priesthood…

to.title: Hebrews 8 New Covenant Better
to.body: # Hebrews 8: A Better New Covenant ## NT Text — Heb 8:6-13 Christ's ministry is more excellent because it mediates a better covenant. The author quotes Jeremiah 31 in full — laws on hearts, knowledge of the Lord, sins remembered no more. ## OT Antecedents - **Jer 31:31-34** — Behold the days are coming — I will make a new covenant. - **Ezek 36:26-27** — New heart and Spirit. - **Deut 30:6** — Circ…

from.title: 1 Tim 2 One Mediator
from.body: # 1 Tim 2: One Mediator ## NT Text — 1 Tim 2:5-6 There is one God and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus, who gave himself as a ransom for all. Moses-mediation completed and surpassed. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 32:30-32** — Moses pleads as mediator for Israel. - **Job 9:33** — Would there were a daysman. - **Isa 53:10** — His soul an offering for sin. ## Shared structure - Sin…

to.title: Job Mediator Daysman
to.body: # Job's Cry for a Daysman → Christ Our Mediator ## OT Story — Job 9:32-35; 16:19-21 Job: 'There is no umpire between us… Even now my witness is in heaven; he who testifies for me is on high. Oh, that one might plead for a man with God!' He longs for a daysman to lay his hand on both. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Timothy 2:5: One mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus. - Hebrews 7:25: He alway…

from.title: 1 Peter 3 Baptism Like Flood
from.body: # 1 Peter 3: Baptism Like the Flood ## NT Text — 1 Pet 3:18-22 Eight souls were saved through water in Noah's ark; baptism corresponds to this. The flood typifies the cleansing-and-deliverance of Christian baptism. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 7-8** — Noah's flood and ark. - **Gen 6:5-8** — Wickedness met by judgment. - **Ezek 14:14** — Noah, Daniel, and Job — paradigmatic righteous. ## Shared struct…

to.title: Crossing Red Sea Baptism
to.body: # Crossing the Red Sea → Baptism Into Moses, Baptism Into Christ ## OT Story — Exodus 14 Israel walks through the sea on dry ground; the waters return and drown Pharaoh's army. The old life of slavery is buried in the sea. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Corinthians 10:1-2: 'All were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.' - Romans 6:3-4: Baptized into Christ's death, raised to walk in newness. - …

from.title: Psalm 69 Reproaches Fell On Me
from.body: # Psalm 69 / 'Zeal for Your House Has Consumed Me' → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Psalm 69 The psalmist drowns in deep waters; reproach has broken his heart; they gave him vinegar to drink; zeal for God's house consumes him. ## NT Fulfillment - John 2:17: 'Zeal for your house will consume me' at the temple cleansing. - John 15:25: 'They hated me without cause.' - Romans 15:3: The reproaches of t…

to.title: Wisdom Of Solomon Suffering Righteous
to.body: # Wisdom 2 / The Righteous Mocked → Mockery at the Cross ## OT Story — Wisdom of Solomon 2:12-20 'Let us lie in wait for the righteous man, because he is troublesome to us… He calls himself a child of the Lord. Let us see if his words are true… Let us test him… condemn him to a shameful death.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 27:39-43: 'He trusts in God; let God deliver him… He said, I am the Son of G…

from.title: Cup Of Wrath Cup Of Salvation
from.body: # Cup of Wrath / Cup of Salvation → Christ Drinks Wrath, Gives Salvation ## OT Story — Psalm 75:8; Isaiah 51:17-22; Psalm 116:13 The LORD holds a cup of wrath; the wicked drink it to the dregs (Ps 75). Jerusalem has drunk the cup of his fury (Is 51). The psalmist lifts the cup of salvation (Ps 116). ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 26:39: 'Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass.' - John 18:11: 'S…

from.title: 1 Peter 2 Royal Priesthood
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: Royal Priesthood ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:9-10 You are a chosen race, royal priesthood, holy nation, people for his possession — Exodus 19:6 reapplied to the church. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19:5-6** — Kingdom of priests, holy nation. - **Hos 1:9-10; 2:23** — Not-my-people now my people. - **Isa 43:21** — People formed for myself. ## Shared structure - Exodus identity titles - Hosea rev…

to.title: Hebrews 5 Order Of Melchizedek
to.body: # Hebrews 5: Order of Melchizedek ## NT Text — Heb 5:5-10 Christ does not glorify himself but is appointed by God as high priest after the order of Melchizedek. Psalm 110:4 is the controlling text. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 14:18-20** — Melchizedek priest-king greets Abraham. - **Ps 110:4** — Priest forever after the order of Melchizedek. - **Isa 53:12** — Servant's exaltation through suffering. #…

from.title: Revelation 21 New Jerusalem Descending
from.body: # Revelation 21: New Jerusalem Descending ## NT Text — Rev 21:1-22:5 John sees the new heaven and new earth and the New Jerusalem descending from heaven as a bride adorned. God dwells with men. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 65:17-25** — New heavens and new earth. - **Ezek 40-48** — Vision of restored temple-city. - **Zech 8:3** — I will dwell in Zion. ## Shared structure - New cosmos - City descending…

to.title: Revelation 4 Throne Vision
to.body: # Revelation 4: Throne Vision ## NT Text — Rev 4 John sees the throne with the rainbow, four living creatures, twenty-four elders — gathering Ezekiel 1, Isaiah 6, and Daniel 7 into one vision. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 1** — Four living creatures around the throne. - **Isa 6** — Seraphim and Holy, Holy, Holy. - **Dan 7:9-10** — Ancient of Days enthroned. ## Shared structure - Heavenly throne - Su…

from.title: 1 Peter 2 Living Stones
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: Living Stones ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:4-8 Coming to Christ as the living stone, you yourselves like living stones are being built into a spiritual house. Psalm 118 and Isaiah 28 quoted. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 28:16** — Cornerstone in Zion — quoted. - **Ps 118:22** — Stone the builders rejected — quoted. - **Dan 2:34** — Stone cut without hands. ## Shared structure - Cornerstone - Livi…

to.title: Jacobs Stone Pillow
to.body: # Jacob's Stone Pillow / Anointed Pillar → Christ the Cornerstone ## OT Story — Genesis 28:10-22 Fleeing alone, Jacob sleeps with a stone for a pillow and dreams of a ladder to heaven. Waking, he anoints the stone with oil, names the place Bethel ('house of God'), and vows. ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:51: Jesus to Nathanael — you will see heaven opened and angels ascending and descending on the Son…

from.title: Revelation 16 Bowls Of Wrath
from.body: # Revelation 16: Bowls of Wrath ## NT Text — Rev 16 Seven bowls of wrath are poured — sores, blood, scorching sun, darkness, Euphrates dried. Egyptian plagues climax in cosmic recapitulation. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 7-12** — Egyptian plagues. - **Isa 51:17** — Cup of wrath drained. - **Jer 25:15-29** — Cup of wrath given to nations. ## Shared structure - Bowls poured - Seven completes - Plague-…

to.title: Revelation 18 Babylon Fallen
to.body: # Revelation 18: Babylon Fallen ## NT Text — Rev 18 Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great. Merchants and kings lament her destruction; the saints rejoice. Jeremiah and Ezekiel laments echoed. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 21:9** — Fallen, fallen is Babylon. - **Jer 51** — Detailed Babylon-doom. - **Ezek 27** — Lament over Tyre. ## Shared structure - Fallen-fallen formula - Merchant lament - Saints rejoi…

from.title: Sealing Of Stone
from.body: # Sealing of the Stone ## NT Text — Matt 27:62-66 Pilate authorizes a guard and seal on the tomb. The precaution becomes evidence: theft cannot explain the empty tomb. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 6:17** — Sealed lions' den. - **Lam 3:53** — Stone over the pit. - **Job 14:13** — Hide me in Sheol. ## Shared structure - Stone over opening - Official seal - Guard posted - Confinement of righteous ## The…

to.title: Stone Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Stone Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2; Matt 21:42 Jacob's pillow-stone at Bethel, the Sinai tablets, the rejected cornerstone, Daniel's stone cut without hands, the living stone of 1 Peter — stones structure the canon. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 28:18** — Jacob's pillar at Bethel. - **Exod 31:18** — Stone tablets. - **Ps 118:22** — Stone the builders rejected. - **Dan 2:34-35** — Stone …

from.title: Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant
from.body: # Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Isaiah 52:13-53:12 The fourth and climactic Servant Song. The Servant is *"despised and rejected"* (53:3), *"pierced for our transgressions, crushed for our iniquities"* (53:5). He is silent before his accusers — *"like a lamb that is led to the slaughter, and like a sheep that before its shearers is silent, so he opened not his mouth"…

to.title: Isaiah 49 Second Servant Song
to.body: # Second Servant Song / Light to Nations → Christ to the Gentiles ## OT Story — Isaiah 49:1-13 'The LORD called me from the womb… It is too light a thing that you should be my servant to raise up the tribes of Jacob; I will make you a light for the nations.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 13:47: Paul cites it as the apostolic commission. - Luke 2:30-32: Simeon — 'a light for revelation to the Gentiles.'…

from.title: Pierced One Zechariah
from.body: # Pierced One (Zechariah 12:10) ## The oracle YHWH pours out a spirit of grace and supplication on Jerusalem so that they look on "me whom they have pierced" and mourn for him as for an only son. ## Date / context Post-exilic oracle of Zechariah, late 6th c. BC, looking to a future day of repentance. ## Fulfillment John applies it to the spear-thrust at the cross (John 19:34-37); Revelation 1:7 us…

to.title: Stricken Shepherd
to.body: # Stricken Shepherd ## The oracle "Awake, O sword, against my shepherd, against the man who stands next to me… strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered." ## Date / context Zechariah, post-exilic Jerusalem, late 6th c. BC. ## Fulfillment Jesus quotes this on the night of his arrest to predict the disciples' flight (Matthew 26:31; Mark 14:27). ## Connects to `[[../Titles/Good-Shepherd]]`…

from.title: Day Of The LORD Amos
from.body: # Day of the LORD (Amos) ## The oracle "Woe to you who desire the day of YHWH! Why would you have the day of YHWH? It is darkness, and not light" — as if one fled from a lion and met a bear. ## Date / context Amos to northern Israel under Jeroboam II, c. 760 BC, in luxurious complacency. ## Fulfillment The day fell upon Samaria in 722 BC at Assyrian hands and continues to be paradigmatic for divin…

to.title: Day Of The LORD Zephaniah
to.body: # Day of the LORD (Zephaniah) ## The oracle "The great day of YHWH is near… a day of wrath, a day of distress and anguish, a day of ruin and devastation, a day of darkness and gloom, a day of clouds and thick darkness." ## Date / context Zephaniah under Josiah, late 7th c. BC, just before Judah's exile. ## Fulfillment The 586 BC fall of Jerusalem inaugurates; the Dies Irae tradition reads the pass…

from.title: Cup Of Cup Of Wrath
from.body: # Cup of the Lord's Wrath ## NT Text — Matt 26:39; Mark 14:36 Jesus prays that the 'cup' pass from him. OT 'cup' imagery is consistently the cup of God's wrath against sin. Christ drinks what his people deserved. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 51:17** — Cup of his wrath, the bowl of staggering. - **Jer 25:15-29** — Cup of wrath given to the nations. - **Ps 75:8** — Cup with foaming wine. ## Shared stru…

to.title: Words From Cross Eli Eli
to.body: # Word from Cross: Eli Eli Lama Sabachthani ## NT Text — Matt 27:46; Mark 15:34 Jesus cries Psalm 22's opening. By invoking the incipit he invokes the whole psalm — its agony and its vindication. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 22:1** — Why have you forsaken me? - **Lam 1:12** — Is it nothing to you? - **Isa 53:10** — It pleased the LORD to crush him. ## Shared structure - Forsakenness expressed - Psalm …

to.title: Thirty Pieces Of Silver
to.body: # Thirty Pieces of Silver ## The oracle The rejected shepherd is weighed out thirty pieces of silver — the price of a slave — which YHWH then orders thrown to the potter in the house of YHWH. ## Date / context Zechariah's shepherd allegory, post-exilic, late 6th c. BC. ## Fulfillment Judas is paid thirty pieces of silver to betray Jesus and later flings them into the temple, with which the priests…

from.title: Burning Bush
from.body: # Burning Bush ## What happens Moses sees a bush ablaze yet not consumed on Mount Horeb. YHWH speaks from the fire, names Himself "I AM," and commissions Moses to deliver Israel from Egypt. ## Typological significance A theophany of holy fire that dwells with but does not destroy — prefiguring God's covenantal presence among an unworthy people. Anticipates Pentecost's tongues of fire and the indes…

to.title: Elijah Mount Carmel Fire
to.body: # Elijah at Mount Carmel ## What happens Elijah challenges 450 prophets of Baal: the god who answers by fire is God. After Baal's silence, YHWH's fire falls on a water-drenched altar and consumes sacrifice, wood, stones, and water. ## Typological significance Decisive theophany of consuming fire that vindicates true worship and exposes false gods — anticipates Pentecost's tongues of fire and the e…

from.title: Bones Of Joseph Carried Out
from.body: # Bones of Joseph Carried Out → Hope of Resurrection ## OT Story — Genesis 50:24-26 Dying in Egypt, Joseph commands: 'God will surely visit you; carry up my bones from here.' Moses takes the bones at the exodus; Joshua buries them in the land. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 11:22: By faith Joseph spoke of the exodus and gave instructions concerning his bones. - Ezekiel 37: Dry bones live by the Spiri…

to.title: Isaiah 26 Resurrection
to.body: # Isaiah 26 / 'Your Dead Shall Live' → Bodily Resurrection ## OT Story — Isaiah 26:19 'Your dead shall live; their bodies shall rise. Awake and sing, you who dwell in the dust! The earth will give birth to the dead.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 5:28-29: All who are in the tombs will hear his voice and come out. - 1 Thessalonians 4:16: The dead in Christ will rise first. - Daniel 12:2: Many who sleep …

from.title: Hebrews 1 Heir Of All Things
from.body: # Hebrews 1: Heir of All Things ## NT Text — Heb 1:1-4 God has spoken in these last days by his Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom also he made the world. Christ as final word and inheritor. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 2:8** — Ask of me, and I will give you the nations. - **Prov 8:22-31** — Wisdom present at creation. - **Deut 32:8-9** — Inheritance allotted. ## Shared structure …

to.title: Voice From Heaven Beloved Son
to.body: # Voice from Heaven: Beloved Son ## NT Text — Matt 3:17; 17:5 At baptism and transfiguration the Father declares, 'This is my beloved Son.' The formula fuses Psalm 2's royal coronation with Isaiah 42's Servant and possibly Genesis 22's 'only/beloved son.' ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 2:7** — Royal son begotten today. - **Isa 42:1** — Chosen in whom my soul delights. - **Gen 22:2** — Take your son, you…

from.title: Boaz Foot Of Cross Threshing
from.body: # Threshing Floor of Boaz → Approaching the Redeemer in Boldness ## OT Story — Ruth 3 At Naomi's instruction, Ruth comes by night to the threshing floor where Boaz sleeps. She lies at his feet and asks him to spread his wing over her. He responds with covenant kindness. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 4:16: Draw near with boldness to the throne of grace. - Romans 5:8: While we were yet sinners, Christ…

to.title: Ruth Genealogy Of David
to.body: # Ruth in David's Line → Gentile in Christ's Line ## OT Story — Ruth 4:18-22 The book closes with a genealogy: Perez to David, through Boaz and Ruth. The Moabite outsider becomes great-grandmother of the king. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 1:5-6: Ruth named in Christ's genealogy. - Galatians 3:8: Scripture foresaw God would justify the Gentiles. - Isaiah 56:6-8: Foreigners joined to the LORD. ## Sha…

from.title: Cities Of Refuge
from.body: # Cities of Refuge → Christ as Refuge ## OT Story — Numbers 35:9-34 Six cities are designated as refuges where one who has killed unintentionally may flee from the avenger of blood. He stays until the death of the high priest, then returns home free. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 6:18: We have fled to take hold of the hope set before us. - Romans 8:1: No condemnation for those in Christ. - Hebrews 7…

to.title: Mercy Seat Atonement
to.body: # Mercy Seat / Hilasterion → Christ Our Propitiation ## OT Story — Exodus 25:17-22; Leviticus 16:14-15 Over the ark sits the kapporet, the mercy seat, flanked by two cherubim. Once a year the high priest sprinkles blood there for the sins of the people. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 3:25: God put forward Christ as a propitiation (hilasterion). - Hebrews 9:5: The cherubim of glory overshadowing the me…

from.title: 2 Cor 5 Reconciliation Through Christ
from.body: # 2 Cor 5: Reconciliation through Christ ## NT Text — 2 Cor 5:18-21 God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not counting trespasses. He made him to be sin for us — the great exchange. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement bearing of iniquity. - **Isa 53:5-6** — Iniquity of us all laid on him. - **Hos 6:1** — He has torn that he may heal — reconciliation pattern. ## Shared s…

to.title: Two Goats Yom Kippur
to.body: # Two Goats of Atonement → Christ's Death and Bearing-Away ## OT Story — Leviticus 16:7-22 On Yom Kippur, two goats: one slaughtered as sin offering, its blood taken behind the veil; the other (the scapegoat) bears confessed sins into the wilderness, never to return. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 9:11-14: Christ entered the holy places by his own blood. - Hebrews 10:11-14: One offering for sins fore…

from.title: Israel As Firstborn
from.body: # Israel My Firstborn → Christ the Firstborn ## OT Story — Exodus 4:22-23 The LORD's word to Pharaoh: 'Israel is my firstborn son. Let my son go that he may serve me. If you refuse, I will kill your firstborn son.' ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 8:29: Firstborn among many brothers. - Colossians 1:15-18: Firstborn of all creation; firstborn from the dead. - Hebrews 12:23: The assembly of the firstborn …

to.title: Levites Set Apart
to.body: # Levites Set Apart in Place of Firstborn → Royal Priesthood ## OT Story — Numbers 3:11-13; 8:14-19 God claims every firstborn of Israel for himself; he takes the Levites instead. They serve at the tabernacle on behalf of the people. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Peter 2:9: A chosen race, a royal priesthood. - Revelation 1:6: He made us a kingdom, priests to his God. - Exodus 19:6: A kingdom of priests an…

from.title: Jonah Three Days
from.body: # Jonah Three Days in the Fish → Three Days in the Tomb This typology is **the only one Jesus himself names explicitly as a sign**. It is therefore exceptionally load-bearing — if any typology is methodologically validated by canonical scripture, this one is. ## OT Story — Jonah 1-2 God calls Jonah to preach repentance to Nineveh, the capital of Israel's enemy Assyria. Jonah flees by ship in the o…

to.title: Empty Tomb
to.body: # Empty Tomb ## NT Text — Matt 28:6; John 20:6-7 The tomb is empty; grave-clothes remain. The body is raised, not stolen. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 16:10** — You will not abandon my soul to Sheol. - **Hos 6:2** — Third day he will raise us. - **Isa 53:10-11** — He shall see his offspring. ## Shared structure - Tomb opened - Linens left - No body - Angelic announcement ## Theological motif Three-day…

from.title: Centurions Confession
from.body: # Centurion's Confession ## NT Text — Matt 27:54; Mark 15:39 A Gentile centurion confesses Jesus as 'Son of God.' The first post-cross confession comes from outside Israel. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 2:7-8** — Son of God to whom nations given. - **Isa 49:6** — Light to nations. - **Josh 2** — Rahab confesses Israel's God. ## Shared structure - Gentile witness - Sonship confession - First post-cross …

from.body: # Smitten by God ## The oracle "We esteemed him stricken, smitten by God, and afflicted. But he was pierced for our transgressions; upon him was the chastisement that brought us peace." ## Date / context Isaiah 53, exilic horizon; the chastened reflection of those who had misjudged the Servant. ## Fulfillment The cry of dereliction (Matthew 27:46) and the Petrine commentary (1 Peter 2:24) read the…

from.title: Joseph Sold By Brothers
from.body: # Joseph Sold by His Brothers → The Betrayal and Vindication of Christ ## OT Story — Genesis 37, 40, 41-50 Joseph is the **beloved son** of Jacob, given a special garment (the "coat of many colors"). He has prophetic dreams in which his brothers and parents bow down to him. His brothers hate him and plot to kill him; instead, on Judah's suggestion, they sell him for **twenty pieces of silver** to …

to.title: Melchizedek
to.body: # Melchizedek → Christ as Eternal King-Priest A typology developed at length in **Hebrews 7**, anchored on a single verse from Psalm 110 and a brief Genesis episode. The argument is structural: Melchizedek's *absence* of genealogy, his combination of *kingly and priestly* offices, and his appearance *before the Levitical priesthood existed* together prefigure Christ. ## OT Story — Genesis 14:17-20…

from.title: 1 Tim 2 One Mediator
from.body: # 1 Tim 2: One Mediator ## NT Text — 1 Tim 2:5-6 There is one God and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus, who gave himself as a ransom for all. Moses-mediation completed and surpassed. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 32:30-32** — Moses pleads as mediator for Israel. - **Job 9:33** — Would there were a daysman. - **Isa 53:10** — His soul an offering for sin. ## Shared structure - Sin…

from.title: eirene
from.body: # eirene (εἰρήνη) — peace ## Semantic field Peace — in Greek primarily absence of war; in biblical Greek expanded by shalom to include wholeness, well-being, reconciled relationship. ## Theological weight "He Himself is our eirene" (Eph 2:14). Christ is both the eirene-maker and the eirene itself. Justification yields eirene with God (Rom 5:1). ## Septuagint mapping Renders Hebrew shalom. ## Embed…

to.title: shalom
to.body: # shalom (שָׁלוֹם) — peace / wholeness ## Semantic field Complete well-being in every dimension — relational, physical, social, spiritual. Not merely absence of conflict but the flourishing of all things in right order. ## Theological weight Shalom is the goal of covenant and the substance of eschatological hope. The Prince of Shalom (Isa 9:6) restores what sin shattered; His chastisement secures …

from.title: Psalm 16 Holy One Not Decay
from.body: # Psalm 16 / 'You Will Not Let Your Holy One See Decay' → Resurrection Predicted ## OT Story — Psalm 16 David sings: 'I have set the LORD always before me… you will not abandon my soul to Sheol or let your holy one see corruption. You will show me the path of life.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 2:25-32: Peter — David died and was buried; he prophesied Christ's resurrection. - Acts 13:35-37: Paul — the…

to.title: Psalm 23 Shepherd
to.body: # Psalm 23 / The LORD My Shepherd → Christ the Good Shepherd ## OT Story — Psalm 23 'The LORD is my shepherd, I shall not want… he restores my soul… valley of the shadow of death… your rod and staff comfort me… you prepare a table before me.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 10:11: I am the good shepherd; the good shepherd lays down his life. - Revelation 7:17: The Lamb at the center of the throne will sh…

from.title: Joseph In The Pit
from.body: # Joseph in the Pit → Descent and Deliverance ## OT Story — Genesis 37:18-24 The brothers strip Joseph of his ornate robe and cast him into an empty cistern in the wilderness. From the pit he is drawn up and sold into Egypt. ## NT Fulfillment - Psalm 88:6: 'You have put me in the depths of the pit' — lament that anticipates Christ's descent. - Acts 2:24: Death could not hold him. - Ephesians 4:9-1…

to.title: Joseph Of Arimathea Tomb
to.body: # Joseph of Arimathea's New Tomb ## NT Text — Matt 27:57-60 A wealthy disciple buries Jesus in his own new tomb, fulfilling 'with a rich man in his death.' ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:9** — With a rich man in death. - **Gen 23** — Abraham buys tomb for Sarah. - **2 Chr 35:24** — Honorable burial of righteous king. ## Shared structure - Wealthy benefactor - New tomb - Honorable burial - Fulfillmen…

from.title: Everlasting Covenant
from.body: # Everlasting Covenant ## What it is A recurring biblical descriptor (berit olam) applied to several covenants — Noahic, Abrahamic, Davidic, and the eschatological new covenant — emphasizing their enduring, non-revocable character. ## Key elements - Eternal duration (olam) - Applied across multiple specific covenants - Often paired with sworn divine oath - Anchored in God's unchanging character - …

to.title: Salt Covenant
to.body: # Salt Covenant ## What it is A covenant idiom signifying perpetuity and incorruptibility, applied to priestly portions and to the Davidic kingship, signaling an unbreakable, preserved relationship. ## Key elements - Salt added to every grain offering - Priestly portions as "covenant of salt forever" - Davidic kingship over Israel by covenant of salt - Symbol of preservation and purity - Marker of…

from.title: 2 Thess 2 Man Of Lawlessness
from.body: # 2 Thess 2: Man of Lawlessness ## NT Text — 2 Thess 2:1-12 Before the Lord's coming, the man of lawlessness must be revealed — seating himself in God's temple, opposing every god. Echoes of Daniel and Ezekiel's tyrant figures. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 11:36** — King who exalts himself above every god. - **Ezek 28:2** — Prince of Tyre claiming divinity. - **Isa 14:13-14** — Daystar exalting his t…

to.title: Revelation 13 Beast From Sea
to.body: # Revelation 13: Beast from the Sea ## NT Text — Rev 13:1-10 A beast rises from the sea — Daniel 7's four beasts combined into one. Political tyranny absorbs the world's worship. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:2-7** — Four beasts from the sea. - **Ps 74:13-14** — Heads of leviathan crushed. - **Isa 27:1** — Leviathan, the twisting serpent. ## Shared structure - Sea origin - Many heads/horns - Blasphe…

from.title: Hebrews 12 Looking To Jesus
from.body: # Hebrews 12: Looking to Jesus ## NT Text — Heb 12:1-3 Lay aside every weight; run with endurance the race, looking to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of faith. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 16:8** — I have set the LORD before me. - **Isa 50:7** — I have set my face like a flint. - **Prov 4:25** — Let your eyes look directly forward. ## Shared structure - Weight removed - Sin discarded - Endurance rac…

to.title: Hebrews 13 Outside The Camp
to.body: # Hebrews 13: Outside the Camp ## NT Text — Heb 13:11-14 Jesus suffered outside the gate to sanctify the people through his own blood. Let us go to him outside the camp, bearing his reproach. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16:27** — Sin-offering carcasses burned outside the camp. - **Num 19:3** — Red heifer slain outside the camp. - **Exod 33:7** — Tent of meeting pitched outside the camp. ## Shared st…

from.title: Words From Cross Finished
from.body: # Word from Cross: It Is Finished ## NT Text — John 19:30 Jesus cries 'tetelestai' — accomplished. The single word echoes Genesis 2's creation completion and Exodus 39's tabernacle completion. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:1-3** — Heavens and earth finished. - **Exod 39:32** — Work of tabernacle finished. - **Isa 53:11** — By his knowledge make many righteous. ## Shared structure - Completion declar…

to.title: Words From Cross Into Your Hands
to.body: # Word from Cross: Into Your Hands I Commit ## NT Text — Luke 23:46 Jesus dies praying Psalm 31:5 — a Davidic prayer of trust. Stephen later echoes it. Death is recast as commendation. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 31:5** — Into your hand I commit my spirit. - **Eccl 12:7** — Spirit returns to God. - **Acts 7:59** — Stephen echoes this prayer. ## Shared structure - Spirit-commendation - Trust in Father…

from.title: 1 Cor 5 Christ Our Passover
from.body: # 1 Cor 5: Christ Our Passover ## NT Text — 1 Cor 5:7-8 Paul calls Christ 'our Passover lamb, sacrificed for us,' and exhorts cleansing of old leaven. Passover and Unleavened Bread become ethical patterns. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — Passover ordinance. - **Exod 13:6-7** — Unleavened bread for seven days. - **Deut 16:1-8** — Passover-Unleavened Bread combined. ## Shared structure - Lamb slai…

to.title: Lamb Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Lamb Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 1:29; Rev 5 Abel's firstborn, Abraham's substitute, the Passover lamb, the daily tamid, Isaiah's silent lamb, John's 'Lamb of God', Revelation's slain Lamb. The lamb is canon's central animal. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 4:4** — Abel's firstborn lamb. - **Gen 22:8** — God will provide himself a lamb. - **Exod 12** — Passover lamb. - **Isa 53:7** — Like a…

from.title: Joseph In Prison With Two
from.body: # Joseph Between Two Prisoners → Christ Between Two Thieves ## OT Story — Genesis 40 In prison Joseph attends two of Pharaoh's officers — cupbearer and baker. He interprets their dreams: one will be lifted up to life, the other lifted up to death. Both happen on the third day. ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 23:32-43: Christ crucified between two criminals — one to life, one to death. - John 6:35,53-58: …

to.title: Joseph Sold For Silver
to.body: # Joseph Sold for Silver → Christ Sold for Silver ## OT Story — Genesis 37:23-28 Joseph's brothers strip him, throw him in a pit, and sell him to Ishmaelite traders for twenty pieces of silver. They dip his robe in goat's blood to deceive their father. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 26:14-16: Judas sells Jesus for thirty pieces of silver. - Zechariah 11:12-13: Thirty pieces of silver weighed out as p…

from.title: David Anointed Youngest
from.body: # David Anointed in Bethlehem → Christ Anointed ## OT Story — 1 Samuel 16:1-13 Samuel comes to Bethlehem. Jesse parades seven sons; God chooses none. David, the youngest, is summoned from the sheep. Samuel anoints him; the Spirit rushes upon him. ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 13:22: God raised David to be king, a man after his own heart. - Luke 4:18: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me; he has anointed m…

to.title: Micah Bethlehem Birthplace
to.body: # Micah 5:2 / Bethlehem → Christ's Birthplace ## OT Story — Micah 5:2 'But you, O Bethlehem Ephrathah, who are too little to be among the clans of Judah, from you shall come forth for me one who is to be ruler in Israel, whose coming forth is from of old, from ancient days.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 2:1-6: Chief priests cite Micah to Herod. - John 7:42: The crowd knows Christ comes from Bethleh…

from.title: Cup Of Cup Of Wrath
from.body: # Cup of the Lord's Wrath ## NT Text — Matt 26:39; Mark 14:36 Jesus prays that the 'cup' pass from him. OT 'cup' imagery is consistently the cup of God's wrath against sin. Christ drinks what his people deserved. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 51:17** — Cup of his wrath, the bowl of staggering. - **Jer 25:15-29** — Cup of wrath given to the nations. - **Ps 75:8** — Cup with foaming wine. ## Shared stru…

to.title: Sweat As Blood
to.body: # Sweat as Blood ## NT Text — Luke 22:44 Luke records that Jesus' sweat became 'like great drops of blood' in Gethsemane. The detail echoes Adam's curse of toilsome sweat and anticipates the cross's bloodshed; the new Adam labors in the garden of obedience. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 3:19** — By the sweat of your face you shall eat bread. - **Lev 17:11** — Life is in the blood. - **Ps 22:14** — I a…

from.title: Feast Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Feast Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Matt 22; Luke 14; Rev 19 From Passover to Sinai's covenant meal, to Isaiah 25's feast on the mountain, to the Last Supper, to the marriage supper — feasts are eschatological signs. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — Passover meal. - **Exod 24:9-11** — Covenant meal on Sinai. - **Isa 25:6** — Feast of fat things on this mountain. ## Shared structure - Shared…

to.title: Garden Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Garden Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 18-20; Rev 22 Eden, Song of Songs' enclosed garden, Gethsemane, the resurrection garden, the city-garden of Revelation 22 — gardens trace the arc of communion lost, recovered, consummated. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2-3** — Eden garden. - **Song 4:12-16** — Sealed garden of love. - **Isa 51:3** — Wilderness made like Eden. - **Ezek 36:35** — Land beco…

from.title: Great Commission
from.body: # Great Commission ## NT Text — Matt 28:16-20 Jesus on a Galilean mountain commissions disciples to make disciples of all nations. Daniel-7 authority enacted in mission. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:13-14** — All peoples should serve him. - **Gen 12:3** — In you all nations blessed. - **Isa 49:6** — Light to nations. ## Shared structure - Mountain - Universal authority - Universal mission - Trinita…

to.title: Temptation On Mountain
to.body: # Temptation on the Mountain: Kingdoms Offered ## NT Text — Matt 4:8-10; Luke 4:5-8 Satan offers Jesus the kingdoms of the world for an act of worship; Jesus answers from Deuteronomy. The episode echoes Moses on Pisgah viewing the land, but here a usurper claims to grant it. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 6:13** — Worship the LORD alone — Jesus' citation. - **Deut 34:1-4** — Moses sees the kingdoms fr…

from.title: Wisdom Of Solomon Suffering Righteous
from.body: # Wisdom 2 / The Righteous Mocked → Mockery at the Cross ## OT Story — Wisdom of Solomon 2:12-20 'Let us lie in wait for the righteous man, because he is troublesome to us… He calls himself a child of the Lord. Let us see if his words are true… Let us test him… condemn him to a shameful death.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 27:39-43: 'He trusts in God; let God deliver him… He said, I am the Son of G…

to.title: Words From Cross Eli Eli
to.body: # Word from Cross: Eli Eli Lama Sabachthani ## NT Text — Matt 27:46; Mark 15:34 Jesus cries Psalm 22's opening. By invoking the incipit he invokes the whole psalm — its agony and its vindication. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 22:1** — Why have you forsaken me? - **Lam 1:12** — Is it nothing to you? - **Isa 53:10** — It pleased the LORD to crush him. ## Shared structure - Forsakenness expressed - Psalm …

from.title: Psalm 69 Reproaches Fell On Me
from.body: # Psalm 69 / 'Zeal for Your House Has Consumed Me' → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Psalm 69 The psalmist drowns in deep waters; reproach has broken his heart; they gave him vinegar to drink; zeal for God's house consumes him. ## NT Fulfillment - John 2:17: 'Zeal for your house will consume me' at the temple cleansing. - John 15:25: 'They hated me without cause.' - Romans 15:3: The reproaches of t…

to.title: Gall And Vinegar
to.body: # Gall and Vinegar ## The oracle "They gave me poison for food, and for my thirst they gave me sour wine to drink" (Psalm 69:21). ## Date / context Davidic lament psalm, frequently cited in passion narratives. ## Fulfillment Jesus is offered wine mixed with gall and, later, sour wine on a sponge; John notes Jesus says "I thirst" so that Scripture might be fulfilled (John 19:28). ## Connects to `[[…

from.title: Hebrews 1 Heir Of All Things
from.body: # Hebrews 1: Heir of All Things ## NT Text — Heb 1:1-4 God has spoken in these last days by his Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom also he made the world. Christ as final word and inheritor. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 2:8** — Ask of me, and I will give you the nations. - **Prov 8:22-31** — Wisdom present at creation. - **Deut 32:8-9** — Inheritance allotted. ## Shared structure …

to.title: Hebrews 9 Mediator Of New Covenant
to.body: # Hebrews 9: Mediator of the New Covenant ## NT Text — Heb 9:15-22 Christ is mediator of a new covenant. Where a covenant exists, the death of the covenant-maker must be established. Sinai's blood-sprinkling foreshadowed Calvary's. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 24:6-8** — Moses sprinkles blood on the people. - **Jer 31:33** — New covenant. - **Gen 15:9-21** — Covenant-cutting with Abraham. ## Shared …

from.title: Refiners Fire Messiah
from.body: # Refiner's Fire Messiah ## The oracle "Behold, I send my messenger… and the Lord whom you seek will suddenly come to his temple"; he is like a refiner's fire and fuller's soap, purifying the sons of Levi. ## Date / context Malachi, late post-exilic Judah, c. 430 BC. ## Fulfillment Jesus identifies the forerunner-messenger with John the Baptist (Matthew 11:10); his temple cleansings enact the sudd…

to.title: Sun Of Righteousness
to.body: # Sun of Righteousness ## The oracle For those who fear YHWH's name, the sun of righteousness shall rise with healing in its wings, and they shall go out leaping like calves from the stall. ## Date / context Malachi, last book of the Hebrew canon, c. 430 BC, closing the OT with eschatological dawn. ## Fulfillment Zechariah's Benedictus echoes the image — "the sunrise shall visit us from on high" —…

from.title: Psalm 8 Son Of Man
from.body: # Psalm 8 / Son of Man Crowned → Christ's Dominion Restored ## OT Story — Psalm 8 'What is man that you are mindful of him, the son of man that you care for him?' Made a little lower than the heavenly beings, crowned with glory, all things under his feet. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 2:5-9: Applied to Jesus — we see Jesus crowned with glory and honor. - 1 Corinthians 15:27: All things put in subjec…

to.title: Revelation 1 Son Of Man Vision
to.body: # Revelation 1: Son of Man Vision ## NT Text — Rev 1:12-18 John sees one 'like a son of man' among golden lampstands — eyes like fire, feet like burnished bronze, voice like many waters. Daniel 7 and 10 are gathered. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:13** — One like a son of man. - **Dan 10:5-6** — Man clothed in linen, eyes like fire. - **Ezek 1:7** — Feet like burnished bronze. ## Shared structure - A…

from.title: Psalm 23 Shepherd
from.body: # Psalm 23 / The LORD My Shepherd → Christ the Good Shepherd ## OT Story — Psalm 23 'The LORD is my shepherd, I shall not want… he restores my soul… valley of the shadow of death… your rod and staff comfort me… you prepare a table before me.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 10:11: I am the good shepherd; the good shepherd lays down his life. - Revelation 7:17: The Lamb at the center of the throne will sh…

to.title: Psalm 72 Royal Reign
to.body: # Psalm 72 / Solomon's Reign → Messiah's Universal Just Reign ## OT Story — Psalm 72 A psalm of Solomon: he judges with righteousness, defends the poor, kings of Sheba bring gifts, all nations are blessed in him, his name endures forever. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 2:11: Magi bring gold, frankincense, myrrh — echo of Sheba. - Revelation 21:24-26: Kings bring glory into the city. - Galatians 3:14:…

from.title: 1 Cor 15 Firstfruits Of Resurrection
from.body: # 1 Cor 15: Firstfruits of Resurrection ## NT Text — 1 Cor 15:20-23 Christ is the 'firstfruits' of those who sleep. The Levitical firstfruits offering guarantees the rest of the harvest. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 23:9-14** — Sheaf of firstfruits waved before the LORD. - **Deut 26** — Firstfruits ritual. - **Exod 23:19** — Best firstfruits brought to God's house. ## Shared structure - Firstfruits o…

to.title: 1 Peter 1 Living Hope
to.body: # 1 Peter 1: Living Hope ## NT Text — 1 Pet 1:3-9 Peter blesses God for a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ. The inheritance is imperishable, undefiled, unfading, kept in heaven. ## OT Antecedents - **Num 18:20** — LORD as inheritance. - **Ps 16:5-6** — The LORD is my chosen portion. - **Dan 12:2-3** — Resurrection inheritance. ## Shared structure - New birth - Living hope - Imp…

from.title: 2 Cor 5 Reconciliation Through Christ
from.body: # 2 Cor 5: Reconciliation through Christ ## NT Text — 2 Cor 5:18-21 God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not counting trespasses. He made him to be sin for us — the great exchange. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement bearing of iniquity. - **Isa 53:5-6** — Iniquity of us all laid on him. - **Hos 6:1** — He has torn that he may heal — reconciliation pattern. ## Shared s…

to.title: Hebrews 10 Sacrifices Cannot Take Away Sin
to.body: # Hebrews 10: Sacrifices Cannot Take Away Sin ## NT Text — Heb 10:1-14 The law's repeated sacrifices could never make worshippers perfect. Christ's single offering perfects forever those being sanctified. Psalm 40 is the controlling text. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 40:6-8** — Sacrifice and offering you did not desire — a body you prepared. - **Isa 1:11** — I have had enough of burnt offerings. - **1…

from.title: Ezekiel 10 Glory Departs
from.body: # Glory Departs the Temple → Christ Departs the Temple ## OT Story — Ezekiel 10-11 The glory of the LORD lifts from the cherubim, moves to the threshold, and finally to the mountain east of the city. The temple is left desolate before destruction. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 23:38-24:2: 'Your house is left to you desolate'; not one stone left. - Luke 21:5-6: Foretelling the temple's fall. - Mark 1…

to.title: Veil Of Temple Torn
to.body: # Veil of the Temple Torn ## NT Text — Matt 27:51 At Christ's death the temple veil tears top to bottom. The barrier between holy and most-holy is rent. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 26:31-33** — Veil separating holy from most-holy. - **Lev 16:2** — Aaron enters only on Day of Atonement. - **Isa 25:7** — He will destroy the covering. ## Shared structure - Top-down tearing - Sanctuary access - End of …

from.title: Calling Of Twelve
from.body: # Calling of the Twelve ## NT Text — Mark 3:13-19; Luke 6:12-16 Jesus ascends a mountain and appoints twelve apostles. The number is constitutive: a new Israel is being formed around a new lawgiver. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 49** — Twelve patriarchs. - **Exod 24:4** — Twelve pillars at Sinai. - **Josh 4:1-9** — Twelve stones from the Jordan. ## Shared structure - Twelve representatives - Mountain …

to.title: Twelve Memorial Stones
to.body: # Twelve Stones from the Jordan → Living Stones / Apostolic Foundation ## OT Story — Joshua 4 One man from each tribe lifts a stone from the Jordan's bed where the priests stood; twelve stones are set up at Gilgal as a memorial. Twelve more are placed in the riverbed. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Peter 2:5: You yourselves like living stones are being built up. - Revelation 21:14: Twelve foundations beari…

from.title: Elisha Double Portion
from.body: # Elisha's Double Portion → Greater Works by the Spirit ## OT Story — 2 Kings 2:9-15 Elisha asks for a double portion of Elijah's spirit. He sees Elijah taken up; the request is granted. Elisha performs twice the miracles of Elijah. ## NT Fulfillment - John 14:12: Greater works than these will you do. - Acts 1:8: You will receive power when the Spirit comes. - Acts 2:17: I will pour out my Spirit …

to.title: Joel Spirit Poured Out
to.body: # Joel 2 / Spirit Poured on All Flesh → Pentecost ## OT Story — Joel 2:28-32 'I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh; your sons and daughters shall prophesy, your old men shall dream dreams… Everyone who calls on the name of the LORD shall be saved.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 2:16-21: Peter quotes Joel in full at Pentecost. - Romans 10:13: Everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved. -…

from.title: Revelation 4 Throne Vision
from.body: # Revelation 4: Throne Vision ## NT Text — Rev 4 John sees the throne with the rainbow, four living creatures, twenty-four elders — gathering Ezekiel 1, Isaiah 6, and Daniel 7 into one vision. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 1** — Four living creatures around the throne. - **Isa 6** — Seraphim and Holy, Holy, Holy. - **Dan 7:9-10** — Ancient of Days enthroned. ## Shared structure - Heavenly throne - Su…

to.title: Revelation 7 Great Multitude
to.body: # Revelation 7: Great Multitude ## NT Text — Rev 7:9-17 A great multitude no one could number, from every nation, tribe, people, language, standing before the throne and the Lamb. Abrahamic promise fulfilled. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 12:3** — All families blessed. - **Gen 22:17** — Stars and sand-multitude. - **Isa 49:6** — Light to the nations. ## Shared structure - Innumerable multitude - Diver…

from.title: Ezekiel 43 Glory Returns
from.body: # Glory Returns from the East → Christ Returns; God Dwells With Us ## OT Story — Ezekiel 43:1-9 Ezekiel sees the glory of the LORD coming from the east — the same direction it had departed — and filling the new temple. 'I will dwell in their midst forever.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 2:1-4: Spirit fills the house with sound from heaven. - Revelation 21:3: God's dwelling is with man. - Matthew 24:27:…

to.title: Revelation 21 New Jerusalem Descending
to.body: # Revelation 21: New Jerusalem Descending ## NT Text — Rev 21:1-22:5 John sees the new heaven and new earth and the New Jerusalem descending from heaven as a bride adorned. God dwells with men. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 65:17-25** — New heavens and new earth. - **Ezek 40-48** — Vision of restored temple-city. - **Zech 8:3** — I will dwell in Zion. ## Shared structure - New cosmos - City descending…

from.title: Empty Tomb
from.body: # Empty Tomb ## NT Text — Matt 28:6; John 20:6-7 The tomb is empty; grave-clothes remain. The body is raised, not stolen. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 16:10** — You will not abandon my soul to Sheol. - **Hos 6:2** — Third day he will raise us. - **Isa 53:10-11** — He shall see his offspring. ## Shared structure - Tomb opened - Linens left - No body - Angelic announcement ## Theological motif Three-day…

to.title: Jonah Three Days In Fish
to.body: # Jonah Three Days in the Fish → Sign of Jonah, Christ's Burial ## OT Story — Jonah 1-2 Jonah, fleeing, is swallowed by a great fish for three days and three nights. From the belly of Sheol he prays; he is vomited out alive to preach to Nineveh. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 12:38-41: As Jonah was three days, so will the Son of Man be. - Luke 11:29-30: The sign of Jonah. - Jonah 2:6: I went down to …

from.title: 1 Peter 2 He Bore Our Sins
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: He Bore Our Sins ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:21-25 Christ left an example; he himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. Isaiah 53 is paraphrased throughout. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:4-6** — He bore our griefs; by his wounds we are healed. - **Isa 53:7** — Silent like a lamb. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear their iniquities. ## Shared struct…

to.title: Hebrews 12 Looking To Jesus
to.body: # Hebrews 12: Looking to Jesus ## NT Text — Heb 12:1-3 Lay aside every weight; run with endurance the race, looking to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of faith. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 16:8** — I have set the LORD before me. - **Isa 50:7** — I have set my face like a flint. - **Prov 4:25** — Let your eyes look directly forward. ## Shared structure - Weight removed - Sin discarded - Endurance rac…

from.title: Centurions Confession
from.body: # Centurion's Confession ## NT Text — Matt 27:54; Mark 15:39 A Gentile centurion confesses Jesus as 'Son of God.' The first post-cross confession comes from outside Israel. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 2:7-8** — Son of God to whom nations given. - **Isa 49:6** — Light to nations. - **Josh 2** — Rahab confesses Israel's God. ## Shared structure - Gentile witness - Sonship confession - First post-cross …

to.title: Trial Before Caiaphas
to.body: # Trial Before Caiaphas ## NT Text — Matt 26:57-68 The Sanhedrin condemns Jesus when he identifies himself with Daniel 7's Son of Man coming on clouds and Psalm 110's enthroned Lord. The judges become the judged. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:13-14** — Son of Man on the clouds. - **Ps 110:1** — Sit at my right hand. - **Isa 53:7** — Silent like a lamb. ## Shared structure - False witnesses - Silent …

from.title: Hebrews 2 Tasted Death For Everyone
from.body: # Hebrews 2: Tasted Death for Everyone ## NT Text — Heb 2:9-18 Jesus, made lower than angels for a little while, tasted death for everyone. He is a merciful and faithful high priest because he shared in our flesh. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 8** — Made lower than the heavenly beings. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear iniquities. - **Job 19:25** — Redeemer who stands. ## Shared structure - Incarnation as solidar…

to.title: Philippians 2 Christ Hymn Kenosis
to.body: # Philippians 2: Christ Hymn Kenosis ## NT Text — Phil 2:5-11 Christ, though in the form of God, emptied himself, taking the form of a servant, becoming obedient to death — even death on a cross — and was exalted to receive every knee's bow. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 45:23** — Every knee shall bow to me — quoted. - **Isa 52:13-53:12** — Servant abased and exalted. - **Dan 7:13-14** — Universal dom…

from.title: Doubting Thomas
from.body: # Doubting Thomas ## NT Text — John 20:24-29 Thomas demands to touch the wounds and confesses 'my Lord and my God.' Highest Johannine confession follows deepest doubt. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 35:23** — My God and my Lord. - **Zech 12:10** — Look on him whom they pierced. - **Isa 53:5** — By his wounds we are healed. ## Shared structure - Demand for evidence - Risen body encountered - Wound-venera…

to.title: Road To Emmaus
to.body: # Road to Emmaus ## NT Text — Luke 24:13-32 The risen Christ walks with two disciples and opens 'Moses and all the prophets' to show the necessity of messianic suffering. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 18:15** — Prophet like Moses. - **Isa 53** — Suffering Servant. - **Ps 22; Ps 16** — Suffering and resurrection psalms. ## Shared structure - Walking journey - Stranger reveals Scripture - Hearts burnin…

from.title: 1 Cor 10 Rock Was Christ
from.body: # 1 Cor 10: The Rock Was Christ ## NT Text — 1 Cor 10:1-13 Paul reads Israel's wilderness experiences typologically: cloud, sea, spiritual food and drink — 'and the Rock was Christ.' Sacrament-shaped warning. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 14** — Cloud and sea. - **Exod 17** — Water from the rock. - **Num 20** — Second rock-incident. ## Shared structure - Cloud/sea as baptism - Manna as spiritual food…

to.title: Stone Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Stone Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2; Matt 21:42 Jacob's pillow-stone at Bethel, the Sinai tablets, the rejected cornerstone, Daniel's stone cut without hands, the living stone of 1 Peter — stones structure the canon. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 28:18** — Jacob's pillar at Bethel. - **Exod 31:18** — Stone tablets. - **Ps 118:22** — Stone the builders rejected. - **Dan 2:34-35** — Stone …

from.title: Crucifixion Between Two Thieves
from.body: # Crucifixion Between Two Thieves ## NT Text — Luke 23:32-43 Jesus is crucified between two criminals, fulfilling Isaiah's 'numbered with the transgressors.' One mocks, one believes — a microcosm of human response. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:12** — Numbered with the transgressors. - **Ps 22:16** — Pierce my hands and feet. - **Zech 13:7** — Strike the shepherd. ## Shared structure - Three crosse…

to.title: Doubting Thomas
to.body: # Doubting Thomas ## NT Text — John 20:24-29 Thomas demands to touch the wounds and confesses 'my Lord and my God.' Highest Johannine confession follows deepest doubt. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 35:23** — My God and my Lord. - **Zech 12:10** — Look on him whom they pierced. - **Isa 53:5** — By his wounds we are healed. ## Shared structure - Demand for evidence - Risen body encountered - Wound-venera…

from.title: Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant
from.body: # Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Isaiah 52:13-53:12 The fourth and climactic Servant Song. The Servant is *"despised and rejected"* (53:3), *"pierced for our transgressions, crushed for our iniquities"* (53:5). He is silent before his accusers — *"like a lamb that is led to the slaughter, and like a sheep that before its shearers is silent, so he opened not his mouth"…

to.title: Zechariah 12 They Will Look On Him
to.body: # They Will Look on Him Whom They Pierced → John 19; Revelation 1 ## OT Story — Zechariah 12:10 'I will pour out on the house of David a spirit of grace and pleas for mercy, so that when they look on me, on him whom they have pierced, they shall mourn.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 19:34-37: Soldier pierced his side; Scripture fulfilled. - Revelation 1:7: Every eye will see him, even those who pierced…

from.title: Micah Ruler From Old
from.body: # Micah 5 / Ruler From Old → Eternal Christ ## OT Story — Micah 5:2-5 'His goings forth are from of old, from ancient of days… He shall stand and shepherd his flock… And he shall be their peace.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:1: In the beginning was the Word. - Hebrews 13:8: Jesus Christ the same yesterday, today, and forever. - Ephesians 2:14: He himself is our peace. ## Shared structure - Pre-exist…

to.title: Psalm 23 Shepherd
to.body: # Psalm 23 / The LORD My Shepherd → Christ the Good Shepherd ## OT Story — Psalm 23 'The LORD is my shepherd, I shall not want… he restores my soul… valley of the shadow of death… your rod and staff comfort me… you prepare a table before me.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 10:11: I am the good shepherd; the good shepherd lays down his life. - Revelation 7:17: The Lamb at the center of the throne will sh…

from.title: Bread Of Life

to.body: # The Bread of Life Discourse ## What happens After the feeding of the five thousand, Jesus declares 'I am the bread of life'; those who eat His flesh and drink His blood have eternal life. Eucharistic flesh-and-blood language sharpens the scandal: the bread is a person who must be consumed by faith. Sif…

from.title: Forty Days In Wilderness
from.body: # Forty Days in the Wilderness ## NT Text — Matt 4:1-2; Mark 1:12-13; Luke 4:1-2 Jesus fasts forty days in the wilderness, recapitulating Israel's forty years, Moses' two forty-day fasts on Sinai, and Elijah's forty-day journey to Horeb.
