from.body: # Hezekiah's Sundial → Time Reversed as Sign of Healing ## OT Story — 2 Kings 20:8-11 As a sign that Hezekiah will be healed, the shadow on the sundial moves backward ten steps. God interrupts the irreversible. ## NT Fulfillment - John 11:25: I am the resurrection. - 1 Corinthians 15:54-57: Death is swallowed up in victory. - Joshua 10:13: Sun standing still — analogous sign. ## Shared structure -…

You shall go out leaping like calves from the stall.' ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 1:78-79: The dayspring from on high has visited us. - Revelation 1:16: His face shining like the sun. - Matthew 13:43: The righteous will s…

from.body: # Baptism of Jesus as Theophany ## NT Text — Matt 3:13-17; Mark 1:9-11; Luke 3:21-22 Jesus is baptized in the Jordan, the heavens open, the Spirit descends, and the Father speaks. to.body: # Crossing the Jordan → Entry into Promised Rest ## OT Story — Joshua 3 The priests carrying the ark step into the Jordan at flood; the waters stand in a heap and Israel crosses on dry ground into the land. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 6:3-4: Baptized into Christ's death, raised to walk in newness. - Hebrews 4:8-11: Joshua did not give them final rest; a Sabbath remains. - Matthew 3:13-17: Jesus' ba…

from.title: Darkness Over Land
from.body: # Darkness Over the Land ## NT Text — Matt 27:45 Darkness covers the land from noon to three. The cosmic sign echoes the ninth Egyptian plague and Amos's day-of-the-Lord. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 10:21-23** — Three days darkness over Egypt. - **Amos 8:9** — Sun goes down at noon. - **Joel 2:31** — Sun turned to darkness. ## Shared structure - Daylight extinguished - Cosmic mourning - Plague echo…

from.body: # Word from Cross: Father, Forgive Them ## NT Text — Luke 23:34 Jesus intercedes for his crucifiers, echoing the Servant who 'made intercession for transgressors.' ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:12** — Intercession for transgressors. - **Num 14:19-20** — Moses intercedes for Israel. - **Gen 50:17** — Joseph forgives brothers. ## Shared structure - Intercession amid suffering - Forgiveness offered - …

to.title: Words From Cross Finished
to.body: # Word from Cross: It Is Finished ## NT Text — John 19:30 Jesus cries 'tetelestai' — accomplished. The single word echoes Genesis 2's creation completion and Exodus 39's tabernacle completion. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:1-3** — Heavens and earth finished. - **Exod 39:32** — Work of tabernacle finished. - **Isa 53:11** — By his knowledge make many righteous. ## Shared structure - Completion declar…

from.title: Healing At Beautiful Gate
from.body: # Healing at the Beautiful Gate ## NT Text — Acts 3:1-10 Peter heals a lame man at the temple gate. The healing fulfills Isaiah 35:6 ('the lame shall leap') and enacts kingdom restoration at the temple's threshold. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 35:6** — Then shall the lame leap like a deer. - **Mic 4:6-7** — I will assemble the lame. - **2 Sam 5:8** — David's saying about the lame. ## Shared structure…

to.title: Hezekiahs Healing
to.body: # Hezekiah's Healing → Deliverance from the Pit ## OT Story — 2 Kings 20:1-7 Hezekiah, sick to death, weeps and prays. Isaiah returns: 'I have heard your prayer; I will add fifteen years.' A poultice of figs is applied. ## NT Fulfillment - 2 Corinthians 1:8-10: We had received the sentence of death, that we might trust in God who raises the dead. - Psalm 30:3: You brought up my soul from Sheol. - …

from.title: Crucifixion Between Two Thieves
from.body: # Crucifixion Between Two Thieves ## NT Text — Luke 23:32-43 Jesus is crucified between two criminals, fulfilling Isaiah's 'numbered with the transgressors.' One mocks, one believes — a microcosm of human response. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:12** — Numbered with the transgressors. - **Ps 22:16** — Pierce my hands and feet. - **Zech 13:7** — Strike the shepherd. ## Shared structure - Three crosse…

to.title: Kiss Of Judas
to.body: # Kiss of Judas ## NT Text — Matt 26:47-50; Luke 22:47-48 Judas betrays Jesus with a kiss. The gesture inverts covenant intimacy into treachery, echoing Joab's kiss-and-kill of Amasa and the proverb that an enemy's kisses are deceitful. ## OT Antecedents - **2 Sam 20:9-10** — Joab kisses Amasa and stabs him. - **Ps 41:9** — My close friend who ate my bread lifted his heel — cited in John 13:18. - …

from.title: Jude Contend For Faith
from.body: # Jude: Contend for the Faith ## NT Text — Jude 3-4 Contend for the faith once for all delivered to the saints. The exhortation invokes the prophets' contention against false teachers in Israel. ## OT Antecedents - **Jer 23** — False prophets vs. true. - **Ezek 13** — Whitewashing prophets denounced. - **Mal 2:7-9** — Priests who corrupt the covenant. ## Shared structure - Faith delivered - Once-f…

to.title: Jude Enoch Prophecy
to.body: # Jude: Enoch's Prophecy ## NT Text — Jude 14-15 Enoch prophesied of the Lord coming with thousands of holy ones to execute judgment. Jude cites the antediluvian patriarch as a witness to the parousia. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 5:21-24** — Enoch walked with God and was not. - **Deut 33:2** — LORD came with myriads of holy ones. - **Zech 14:5** — LORD comes with all the holy ones. ## Shared structu…

from.title: Blood Covenant
from.body: # Blood Covenant ## What it is The general scriptural form of covenant ratification through the shedding and application of blood, signaling that the parties bind themselves under the penalty of death for unfaithfulness. ## Key elements - Cutting and passing between pieces (Gen 15) - Sprinkling on people and altar (Ex 24) - Self-maledictory oath - Atonement through life-for-life substitution - Inv…

to.title: Salt Covenant
to.body: # Salt Covenant ## What it is A covenant idiom signifying perpetuity and incorruptibility, applied to priestly portions and to the Davidic kingship, signaling an unbreakable, preserved relationship. ## Key elements - Salt added to every grain offering - Priestly portions as "covenant of salt forever" - Davidic kingship over Israel by covenant of salt - Symbol of preservation and purity - Marker of…

from.title: Psalm 118 Stone Rejected
from.body: # Psalm 118 / Stone the Builders Rejected → Cornerstone ## OT Story — Psalm 118:22-23 'The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. This is the LORD's doing; it is marvelous in our eyes.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 21:42: Jesus applies it to himself. - Acts 4:11: Peter to the Sanhedrin — you are the builders. - 1 Peter 2:7: Cornerstone for believers, stumbling-stone for unbelievers…

to.body: # Psalm 69 / 'Zeal for Your House Has Consumed Me' → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Psalm 69 The psalmist drowns in deep waters; reproach has broken his heart; they gave him vinegar to drink; zeal for God's house consumes him. ## NT Fulfillment - John 2:17: 'Zeal for your house will consume me' at the temple cleansing. - John 15:25: 'They hated me without cause.' - Romans 15:3: The reproaches of t…

from.body: # 1 John 2: Atoning Sacrifice ## NT Text — 1 John 2:1-2 If anyone sins we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous. He is the propitiation for our sins — and for the whole world. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement propitiation. - **Isa 53:10** — Soul an offering for sin. - **Ps 32:1-2** — Blessed is he whose sin is covered. ## Shared structure - Advocate - Propit…

from.title: Lamb Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Lamb Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 1:29; Rev 5 Abel's firstborn, Abraham's substitute, the Passover lamb, the daily tamid, Isaiah's silent lamb, John's 'Lamb of God', Revelation's slain Lamb. The lamb is canon's central animal. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 4:4** — Abel's firstborn lamb. - **Gen 22:8** — God will provide himself a lamb. - **Exod 12** — Passover lamb. - **Isa 53:7** — Like a…

to.title: Last Supper As Passover
to.body: # Last Supper as Passover ## NT Text — Matt 26:17-30; Mark 14:12-26; Luke 22:7-23 The Last Supper is celebrated as a Passover meal, with Jesus reinterpreting bread and cup as his body and blood. The Exodus typology comes to its fulfillment: the true Passover Lamb is at table. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — The Passover meal in Egypt. - **Exod 24:8** — Blood of the covenant sprinkled on the peop…

BC, addressed to a future people who confess their misjudgment of him. ## Fulf…

from.body: # Ashes of the Red Heifer Stored → Continual Cleansing ## OT Story — Numbers 19:1-10 The ashes of the red heifer are stored outside the camp 'for the water of impurity' — used whenever a person becomes defiled by death. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 9:13-14: If the ashes purify the flesh, how much more the blood of Christ. - 1 John 1:9: He is faithful and just to cleanse. - Titus 3:5: The washing of…

to.title: Scapegoat Azazel
to.body: # Scapegoat into the Wilderness → Sin Borne Away ## OT Story — Leviticus 16:20-22 The high priest lays both hands on the live goat, confesses Israel's sins over it, and a fit man leads it to a solitary place. The goat bears all iniquities away. ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:29: The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world. - Isaiah 53:6: The LORD has laid on him the iniquity of us all. - Hebre…

from.title: Revelation 12 Woman Crowned Stars
from.body: # Revelation 12: Woman Crowned with Stars ## NT Text — Rev 12:1-6 A woman clothed with the sun, crown of twelve stars, gives birth to a male child. Joseph's dream of celestial homage rises again, and Israel/the Messianic community is figured. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 37:9-11** — Joseph's sun-moon-stars dream. - **Isa 66:7-9** — Zion's labor and birth. - **Mic 5:3** — Until she who is in travail b…

Psalm 2's enthronement on Zion fulfilled. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 2:6** — I have set my king on Zion, my holy hill. - **Joel 2:32** — Mount Zion shall be refuge. - **Obad 17** — On Mount Zion there shall be deliverance. ## Shared structure - Lamb ent…

from.title: Cain Abel Two Sacrifices
from.body: # Cain & Abel / Two Sacrifices → Christ's Better Blood ## OT Story — Genesis 4:1-16 The first sons of Adam bring offerings. Abel offers the firstborn of his flock with their fat (a blood sacrifice); Cain offers the fruit of the ground. The LORD receives Abel's offering but not Cain's. Cain rises against his righteous brother and murders him; Abel's blood "cries from the ground." ## NT Fulfillment …

Implicit in the act is the death of an animal — the first sacrifice and covering. ## NT Fulfillment - Isaiah 61:10: He has clothed me with the garments of salvation, covered me with the robe of righteousness. - Romans…

to.title: Revelation 3 White Garments
to.body: # Revelation 3: White Garments ## NT Text — Rev 3:4-5 Those who have not soiled their garments will walk with me in white. Conqueror clothed in white — name not blotted from book of life. ## OT Antecedents - **Eccl 9:8** — Let your garments be always white. - **Isa 1:18** — White as snow. - **Zech 3:3-5** — Joshua's filthy garments exchanged for clean. ## Shared structure - Garment imagery - White…

from.title: Parting Of Red Sea
from.body: # Parting of the Red Sea ## What happens Trapped between Pharaoh's army and the sea, Israel passes through walls of divided water on dry ground; the army that pursues is drowned. ## Typological significance Baptismal passage through death-waters into new life — Paul reads it as proto-baptism (1 Cor 10). Patterns the descent-and-emergence of resurrection and the destruction of the pursuing accuser.…

to.body: # Water From the Rock ## What happens At Israel's complaining, Moses strikes (and later speaks to) a rock, from which water gushes for the thirsty congregation. ## Typological significance Paul calls the rock Christ — struck once to release living water. The second incident, where Moses strikes instead of speaking, costs him the land, dramatizing that the Rock is struck only once. ## Connects to `…

from.title: Simon Of Cyrene Helper
from.body: # Simon of Cyrene Bears the Cross ## NT Text — Mark 15:21 Simon of Cyrene is compelled to carry the cross with Jesus. from.title: agape
from.body: # agape (ἀγάπη) — self-giving love ## Semantic field The NT's distinctive love-word — self-giving, willed, covenantal love. The dividing wall is broken; one new humanity is created. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 57:19** — Peace to the far and to the near. - **Isa 56:6-7** — Foreigners brought to my holy mountain. - **Zech 6:15** — Those far off shall come and help build the temple. ## Shared structu…

from.title: Spirit Anointing Isaiah 61
from.body: # Spirit Anointing (Isaiah 61) ## The oracle "The Spirit of the Lord YHWH is upon me, because YHWH has anointed me to bring good news to the poor… to proclaim the year of YHWH's favor." ## Date / context Isaiah's restoration oracles, exilic-postexilic horizon. ## Fulfillment Jesus reads this scroll in the Nazareth synagogue and declares, "Today this Scripture is fulfilled in your hearing" (Luke 4:…

to.title: Spirit Poured Out Joel
to.body: # Spirit Poured Out (Joel) ## The oracle "I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh; your sons and daughters shall prophesy… even on the male and female servants in those days I will pour out my Spirit." ## Date / context Joel's restoration oracle, exact date disputed. ## Fulfillment Peter quotes the passage in full at Pentecost as the explanation for the Spirit's descent on the gathered believers (A…

from.title: Revelation 13 Beast From Sea
from.body: # Revelation 13: Beast from the Sea ## NT Text — Rev 13:1-10 A beast rises from the sea — Daniel 7's four beasts combined into one. Political tyranny absorbs the world's worship. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:2-7** — Four beasts from the sea. - **Ps 74:13-14** — Heads of leviathan crushed. - **Isa 27:1** — Leviathan, the twisting serpent. ## Shared structure - Sea origin - Many heads/horns - Blasphe…

to.title: Revelation 18 Babylon Fallen
to.body: # Revelation 18: Babylon Fallen ## NT Text — Rev 18 Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great. Merchants and kings lament her destruction; the saints rejoice. Jeremiah and Ezekiel laments echoed. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 21:9** — Fallen, fallen is Babylon. - **Jer 51** — Detailed Babylon-doom. - **Ezek 27** — Lament over Tyre. ## Shared structure - Fallen-fallen formula - Merchant lament - Saints rejoi…

from.title: Isaiah 40 Voice Crying
from.body: # Voice in the Wilderness / Prepare the Way → John the Baptist ## OT Story — Isaiah 40:1-11 'Comfort, comfort my people. A voice cries: in the wilderness prepare the way of the LORD. Every valley shall be lifted up.' Glory revealed; all flesh sees together. ## NT Fulfillment - Mark 1:2-3: The opening of the gospel quotes this. - John 1:23: 'I am the voice…' — John's self-identification. - Luke 3:4…

to.title: Zephaniah King In Midst
to.body: # Zephaniah 3 / The King in Your Midst → Christ Dwelling, Singing Over Us ## OT Story — Zephaniah 3:14-17 'Sing aloud, O daughter of Zion! The King of Israel, the LORD, is in your midst… He will rejoice over you with gladness; he will quiet you by his love; he will exult over you with loud singing.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:14: The Word became flesh and dwelt among us. - Matthew 1:23: God with u…

from.title: Stone Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Stone Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2; Matt 21:42 Jacob's pillow-stone at Bethel, the Sinai tablets, the rejected cornerstone, Daniel's stone cut without hands, the living stone of 1 Peter — stones structure the canon. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 28:18** — Jacob's pillar at Bethel. - **Exod 31:18** — Stone tablets. - **Ps 118:22** — Stone the builders rejected. - **Dan 2:34-35** — Stone …

to.title: Tree Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Tree Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Acts 5:30; Gal 3:13; Rev 22 Eden's two trees, Abraham's oaks, Israel's vine and olive, the tree of curse on which Christ hung, the tree of life in Revelation. Trees thread sin and salvation. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:9** — Tree of life and tree of knowledge. - **Gen 21:33** — Abraham's tamarisk. - **Deut 21:23** — Cursed is anyone hung on a tree. - **Ezek…

from.title: Boaz Foot Of Cross Threshing
from.body: # Threshing Floor of Boaz → Approaching the Redeemer in Boldness ## OT Story — Ruth 3 At Naomi's instruction, Ruth comes by night to the threshing floor where Boaz sleeps. She lies at his feet and asks him to spread his wing over her. He responds with covenant kindness. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 4:16: Draw near with boldness to the throne of grace. - Romans 5:8: While we were yet sinners, Christ…

to.title: Mercy Seat Atonement
to.body: # Mercy Seat / Hilasterion → Christ Our Propitiation ## OT Story — Exodus 25:17-22; Leviticus 16:14-15 Over the ark sits the kapporet, the mercy seat, flanked by two cherubim. Once a year the high priest sprinkles blood there for the sins of the people. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 3:25: God put forward Christ as a propitiation (hilasterion). - Hebrews 9:5: The cherubim of glory overshadowing the me…

from.title: Daniel 2 Stone Cut Without Hands
from.body: # Stone Cut Without Hands → Kingdom That Fills the Earth ## OT Story — Daniel 2:31-45 Nebuchadnezzar dreams of a colossus shattered by a stone cut without hands, which becomes a mountain filling the earth. The God of heaven sets up an everlasting kingdom. ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 4:11: The stone rejected. - Luke 20:17-18: The stone falls and crushes. - Matthew 13:31-32: Kingdom grows like a tree. …

to.title: Zechariah 14 Day Of The LORD
to.body: # Day of the LORD / Feet on the Mount of Olives → Christ Returns ## OT Story — Zechariah 14:1-9 'On that day his feet shall stand on the Mount of Olives, which is east of Jerusalem, and the mount shall be split in two… The LORD will be king over all the earth. On that day the LORD will be one and his name one.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 1:11-12: Angels say Jesus will return as he went; the place wa…

from.title: Sirach Wisdom Personified
from.body: # Sirach 24 / Wisdom Pitches Her Tent → Word Tabernacled ## OT Story — Sirach 24 Wisdom says: 'I came forth from the mouth of the Most High… The Creator commanded, Pitch your tent in Jacob. So in Zion I was established.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:14: The Word became flesh and tabernacled among us. - Colossians 2:3: Treasures of wisdom hidden in Christ. - Proverbs 8: Wisdom personified. ## Shared …

to.title: Solomon Wisdom Request
to.body: # Solomon Asks for Wisdom → One Greater Than Solomon ## OT Story — 1 Kings 3:5-15 God appears to Solomon at Gibeon. Solomon asks for a hearing heart to judge the people. God grants wisdom beyond measure, plus riches and honor. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 12:42: Something greater than Solomon is here. - 1 Corinthians 1:30: Christ became to us wisdom from God. - Colossians 2:3: In him are hidden all…

from.title: Samson Solo Victory In Death
from.body: # Samson's Death Brings Greater Victory → Christ Triumphs by Dying ## OT Story — Judges 16:23-31 Blinded, mocked, between two pillars, Samson prays once more. He pushes; the temple collapses; he kills more in his death than in his life. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 2:14-15: Through death he destroyed the one who has the power of death. - Colossians 2:15: He disarmed the powers, triumphing over them…

to.title: Stephens Martyrdom Like Christ
to.body: # Stephen's Martyrdom Patterned on Christ ## NT Text — Acts 7:54-60 Stephen prays 'receive my spirit' and 'do not hold this sin against them' — directly echoing the cross-words of Jesus. The first Christian martyr dies like his Lord. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 31:5** — Into your hand I commit my spirit. - **Isa 53:12** — Intercession for transgressors. - **2 Chr 24:20-22** — Zechariah son of Jehoiad…

from.title: Absalom Rebellion
from.body: # Absalom's Rebellion → Son Rebels Against Father; Cross Subverts ## OT Story — 2 Samuel 15-18 Absalom steals the people's hearts, drives David from Jerusalem. David crosses the Kidron weeping, ascending the Mount of Olives. Eventually Absalom dies hanging in a tree. ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 19:14: 'We do not want this man to reign over us.' - John 18:1: Jesus crossed the Kidron — same path as Dav…

to.title: Crucifixion Between Two Thieves
to.body: # Crucifixion Between Two Thieves ## NT Text — Luke 23:32-43 Jesus is crucified between two criminals, fulfilling Isaiah's 'numbered with the transgressors.' One mocks, one believes — a microcosm of human response. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:12** — Numbered with the transgressors. - **Ps 22:16** — Pierce my hands and feet. - **Zech 13:7** — Strike the shepherd. ## Shared structure - Three crosse…

from.title: Gentiles Streaming To Zion
from.body: # Gentiles Streaming to Zion ## The oracle In the latter days the mountain of YHWH's house shall be established as the highest of mountains, and all nations shall flow to it, saying, "Let us go up to the house of the God of Jacob." ## Date / context Isaiah and Micah, late 8th c. BC; Zechariah picks up the motif after the exile. ## Fulfillment Inaugurated in the gentile mission of the apostles; con…

to.title: New Jerusalem Descending
to.body: # New Jerusalem Descending ## The oracle John sees the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride; God dwells with humanity, every tear wiped away, no more death, mourning, or pain. ## Date / context Climax of Revelation; Ezekiel's temple vision is a major OT antecedent. ## Fulfillment Awaits consummation; foretasted in the church as the city set on a hill. #…

from.title: Barren Fig Tree
from.body: # The Barren Fig Tree ## What happens A vineyard owner orders a barren fig tree cut down after three fruitless years; the vinedresser pleads for one more year of digging and dunging. ## Typological significance Patience under intercession — judgment delayed by mediator's plea. Three fruitless years parallel Jesus' public ministry; the cursed fig tree in Holy Week is this parable enacted. ## Connec…

to.title: Jothams Trees Fable
to.body: # Jotham's Trees Fable ## What happens From Mount Gerizim, Jotham tells how trees sought a king: olive, fig, and vine all refused, leaving only the bramble — which threatens fire to all who do not shelter under it. ## Typological significance The Bible's oldest fable, a withering critique of unworthy kingship. The productive refuse rule; the worthless seize it and threaten fire. Anticipates the th…

from.title: Hebrews 9 Once For All
from.body: # Hebrews 9: Once for All ## NT Text — Heb 9:11-14, 24-28 Christ entered the heavenly sanctuary once for all by his own blood. Annual atonement is superseded by single, eternal redemption. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement — annual. - **Exod 26:33** — Veil before the most-holy. - **Zech 9:11** — Blood of the covenant. ## Shared structure - Heavenly sanctuary - Once-for-all entry - …

to.title: Scapegoat Azazel
to.body: # Scapegoat into the Wilderness → Sin Borne Away ## OT Story — Leviticus 16:20-22 The high priest lays both hands on the live goat, confesses Israel's sins over it, and a fit man leads it to a solitary place. The goat bears all iniquities away. ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:29: The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world. - Isaiah 53:6: The LORD has laid on him the iniquity of us all. - Hebre…

from.title: Mountain Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Mountain Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Heb 12; Matt 5; Rev 21 Mountains structure the canon: Eden's high garden, Moriah, Sinai, Zion, the Mount of Transfiguration, Mount of Olives, the heavenly Mount Zion. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 22** — Moriah. - **Exod 19** — Sinai. - **2 Sam 5:7** — Zion. - **Ezek 28:14** — Holy mountain of God. ## Shared structure - Ascent - Theophany - Covenant - Sacri…

to.title: Tabernacle Pattern From Heaven
to.body: # Tabernacle Built to Heavenly Pattern → Heavenly Sanctuary ## OT Story — Exodus 25:9; 25:40 Moses is to build the tabernacle 'according to the pattern shown you on the mountain.' The earthly is a copy of the heavenly. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 8:5: They serve a copy and shadow of the heavenly things. - Hebrews 9:23-24: Christ entered the true sanctuary, of which the earthly is a copy. - Revelat…

from.title: Crossing Red Sea Baptism
from.body: # Crossing the Red Sea → Baptism Into Moses, Baptism Into Christ ## OT Story — Exodus 14 Israel walks through the sea on dry ground; the waters return and drown Pharaoh's army. The old life of slavery is buried in the sea. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Corinthians 10:1-2: 'All were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.' - Romans 6:3-4: Baptized into Christ's death, raised to walk in newness. - …

to.title: Israel As Firstborn
to.body: # Israel My Firstborn → Christ the Firstborn ## OT Story — Exodus 4:22-23 The LORD's word to Pharaoh: 'Israel is my firstborn son. Let my son go that he may serve me. If you refuse, I will kill your firstborn son.' ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 8:29: Firstborn among many brothers. - Colossians 1:15-18: Firstborn of all creation; firstborn from the dead. - Hebrews 12:23: The assembly of the firstborn …

from.title: 1 Peter 2 Royal Priesthood
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: Royal Priesthood ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:9-10 You are a chosen race, royal priesthood, holy nation, people for his possession — Exodus 19:6 reapplied to the church. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19:5-6** — Kingdom of priests, holy nation. - **Hos 1:9-10; 2:23** — Not-my-people now my people. - **Isa 43:21** — People formed for myself. ## Shared structure - Exodus identity titles - Hosea rev…

from.title: 1 Peter 2 He Bore Our Sins
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: He Bore Our Sins ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:21-25 Christ left an example; he himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. Isaiah 53 is paraphrased throughout. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:4-6** — He bore our griefs; by his wounds we are healed. - **Isa 53:7** — Silent like a lamb. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear their iniquities. ## Shared struct…

to.title: Three Denials Of Peter
to.body: # Three Denials of Peter ## NT Text — Matt 26:69-75; John 18:15-27 Peter denies Jesus three times before the cock crows, as foretold. The threefold structure will be answered by the threefold restoration in John 21. ## OT Antecedents - **2 Sam 12:13** — David's confession after threefold rebuke — pattern of fall and restoration. - **Zech 13:7** — Strike the shepherd and the sheep will be scattered…

from.title: Passover Lamb
from.body: # Passover Lamb → Christ the Paschal Lamb ## OT Story — Exodus 12 The night before the Exodus, God commands every Israelite household to: 1. Select an **unblemished male lamb in its first year** (Ex 12:5) 2. Keep it from the 10th to the 14th of the month — **four days under inspection** 3. Slaughter it at **twilight on the 14th** 4. **Paint its blood on the doorposts and lintel** of the house (Ex …

to.title: Rock Gives Water
to.body: # Rock Gives Water → "The Rock Was Christ" (1 Cor 10:4) ## OT Story — Exodus 17 & Numbers 20 **Exodus 17:1-7** — Israel camps at Rephidim. There is no water. The people quarrel with Moses and *"tested the LORD by saying, 'Is the LORD among us or not?'"* (17:7). God commands Moses: *"Behold, I will stand before you there on **the rock at Horeb**, and you shall **strike the rock**, and water shall c…

from.title: Psalm 22 Crucifixion
from.body: # Psalm 22 → The Crucifixion ## OT Story — Psalm 22 A Davidic lament that pivots from extreme suffering to vindicated praise. The opening cry — *"My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?"* (22:1) — gives way to a sequence of concrete tortures: - *"All who see me mock me; they make mouths at me, they wag their heads"* (22:7) - *"He trusts in the LORD; let him deliver him!"* (22:8) — the mockers' t…

to.title: Lamentations Suffering City
to.body: # Lamentations / Weeping Over the City → Christ's Tears Over Jerusalem ## OT Story — Lamentations 1-3 Jeremiah laments the fallen city: 'How lonely sits the city that was full of people… Is it nothing to you, all you who pass by?' He bears the people's sorrow. ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 19:41-44: Jesus wept over Jerusalem. - Matthew 23:37-39: Lament over Jerusalem. - Lamentations 3:22-23: His mercie…

from.title: Revelation 12 Dragon Cast Down
from.body: # Revelation 12: Dragon Cast Down ## NT Text — Rev 12:7-12 Michael and his angels war against the dragon; the dragon is cast down. The serpent of Eden meets ultimate defeat through the Lamb's blood and the saints' testimony. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 3:14-15** — Serpent crushed by the seed. - **Isa 14:12-15** — Daystar cast down. - **Dan 12:1** — Michael, prince of Israel, arises. ## Shared struct…

to.body: # Revelation 18: Babylon Fallen ## NT Text — Rev 18 Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great. Merchants and kings lament her destruction; the saints rejoice. Jeremiah and Ezekiel laments echoed. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 21:9** — Fallen, fallen is Babylon. - **Jer 51** — Detailed Babylon-doom. - **Ezek 27** — Lament over Tyre. ## Shared structure - Fallen-fallen formula - Merchant lament - Saints rejoi…

from.title: Melchizedek
from.body: # Melchizedek → Christ as Eternal King-Priest A typology developed at length in **Hebrews 7**, anchored on a single verse from Psalm 110 and a brief Genesis episode. The argument is structural: Melchizedek's *absence* of genealogy, his combination of *kingly and priestly* offices, and his appearance *before the Levitical priesthood existed* together prefigure Christ. ## OT Story — Genesis 14:17-20…

to.body: # Rebekah, Bride for Isaac → The Spirit Drawing the Bride of Christ ## OT Story — Genesis 24 Abraham is old. He commissions his **unnamed servant** (traditionally Eliezer of Damascus, Gen 15:2): *"Put your hand under my thigh, that I may make you swear by the LORD ... that you will not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites ... but will go to my country and to my kindred, and …

from.body: # Mountain Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Heb 12; Matt 5; Rev 21 Mountains structure the canon: Eden's high garden, Moriah, Sinai, Zion, the Mount of Transfiguration, Mount of Olives, the heavenly Mount Zion. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 22** — Moriah. - **Exod 19** — Sinai. - **2 Sam 5:7** — Zion. - **Ezek 28:14** — Holy mountain of God. ## Shared structure - Ascent - Theophany - Covenant - Sacri…

to.title: Shepherd Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Shepherd Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 10; Heb 13:20 Abel, Jacob, Moses, David — all shepherds. YHWH as Shepherd in Psalm 23 and Ezekiel 34. Christ as Good Shepherd who lays down his life. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 23** — The LORD is my shepherd. - **Ezek 34** — Faithless shepherds vs. true. - **Zech 13:7** — Strike the shepherd. ## Shared structure - Pastoral vocation - Sheep-guidance -…

from.title: Bathsheba Uriah Sin
from.body: # David's Sin with Bathsheba → Royal Failure and Atoning Need ## OT Story — 2 Samuel 11 King David, idle in spring war-season, takes Bathsheba; she conceives. To cover it, he orders Uriah's death in battle. The thing he had done displeased the LORD. ## NT Fulfillment - Psalm 51: David's penitential prayer. - 1 John 1:9: If we confess, he is faithful and just to forgive. - Matthew 1:6: Listed in ge…

to.title: David Spares Saul
to.body: # David Spares Saul → Christ Forbearing With Persecutors ## OT Story — 1 Samuel 24, 26 Twice David could kill Saul who is hunting him. He refuses: 'I will not put forth my hand against the LORD's anointed.' He waits on God to vindicate. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 12:17-21: Do not avenge yourselves; leave it to the wrath of God. - 1 Peter 2:23: When reviled, he did not revile; he entrusted himself …

To cover it, he orders Uriah's death in battle. The thing he had done displeased the LORD. ## NT Fulfillment - Psalm 51: David's penitential prayer. - 1 John 1:9: If we confess, he is faithful and just to forgive. - Matthew 1:6: Listed in ge…

to.title: Reuben Defiled Bed
to.body: # Reuben Loses the Birthright → Firstborn Privilege Forfeited ## OT Story — Genesis 35:22; 49:3-4 Reuben, the natural firstborn, lies with his father's concubine. In Jacob's deathbed blessing he is told, 'Unstable as water, you shall not have preeminence.' Birthright passes to Joseph, kingship to Judah. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 12:16-17: Like Esau, those who despise the holy lose the inheritanc…

from.title: Revelation 2 Tree Of Life Promise
from.body: # Revelation 2: Tree of Life Promise ## NT Text — Rev 2:7 To the one who conquers I will grant to eat of the tree of life in the paradise of God. Eden's lost tree is promised back. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:9** — Tree of life in midst of garden. - **Gen 3:22-24** — Cherubim guard the tree of life. - **Prov 3:18** — She is a tree of life to those who lay hold of her. ## Shared structure - Promise…

to.title: Revelation 21 New Jerusalem Descending
to.body: # Revelation 21: New Jerusalem Descending ## NT Text — Rev 21:1-22:5 John sees the new heaven and new earth and the New Jerusalem descending from heaven as a bride adorned. God dwells with men. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 65:17-25** — New heavens and new earth. - **Ezek 40-48** — Vision of restored temple-city. - **Zech 8:3** — I will dwell in Zion. ## Shared structure - New cosmos - City descending…

from.title: Revelation 12 Woman Crowned Stars
from.body: # Revelation 12: Woman Crowned with Stars ## NT Text — Rev 12:1-6 A woman clothed with the sun, crown of twelve stars, gives birth to a male child. Joseph's dream of celestial homage rises again, and Israel/the Messianic community is figured. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 37:9-11** — Joseph's sun-moon-stars dream. - **Isa 66:7-9** — Zion's labor and birth. - **Mic 5:3** — Until she who is in travail b…

to.title: Revelation 17 Babylon The Harlot
to.body: # Revelation 17: Babylon the Harlot ## NT Text — Rev 17 A great harlot sits on many waters, drunken with the blood of the saints. Babylon-Tyre prophecies converge. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 47** — Lament over Babylon. - **Jer 50-51** — Babylon's fall. - **Ezek 16; 23** — Faithless harlot Jerusalem. ## Shared structure - Harlot imagery - Sitting on waters - Cup of abominations - Adornment in gold -…

from.title: 1 Thess 4 Coming Trumpet
from.body: # 1 Thess 4: Coming with the Trumpet ## NT Text — 1 Thess 4:13-18 The Lord descends from heaven with a cry of command, the archangel's voice, and the trumpet of God; the dead in Christ rise and the living are caught up. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19:16** — Trumpet at Sinai. - **Isa 27:13** — Great trumpet, scattered gathered. - **Dan 12:1-2** — Michael arises; many awake. ## Shared structure - Tru…

to.title: Revelation 4 Throne Vision
to.body: # Revelation 4: Throne Vision ## NT Text — Rev 4 John sees the throne with the rainbow, four living creatures, twenty-four elders — gathering Ezekiel 1, Isaiah 6, and Daniel 7 into one vision. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 1** — Four living creatures around the throne. - **Isa 6** — Seraphim and Holy, Holy, Holy. - **Dan 7:9-10** — Ancient of Days enthroned. ## Shared structure - Heavenly throne - Su…

from.title: Sermon On Mount New Sinai
from.body: # Sermon on the Mount as New Sinai ## NT Text — Matt 5-7 Jesus ascends a mountain, sits down, and authoritatively teaches his disciples. Matthew structures the scene to evoke Moses at Sinai — but the new lawgiver speaks not 'thus says the LORD' but 'I say to you.' ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19-20** — Moses on Sinai delivers the Decalogue. - **Deut 30:11-14** — Word near you — internalized Torah an…

to.title: Temptation On Mountain
to.body: # Temptation on the Mountain: Kingdoms Offered ## NT Text — Matt 4:8-10; Luke 4:5-8 Satan offers Jesus the kingdoms of the world for an act of worship; Jesus answers from Deuteronomy. The episode echoes Moses on Pisgah viewing the land, but here a usurper claims to grant it. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 6:13** — Worship the LORD alone — Jesus' citation. - **Deut 34:1-4** — Moses sees the kingdoms fr…

from.title: Hanukkah Light Returns
from.body: # Hanukkah / Rededication of the Temple → Christ Speaks at the Feast ## OT Story — 1 Maccabees 4:36-59 After Antiochus' desecration, the Maccabees cleanse and rededicate the temple. The Feast of Dedication (Hanukkah) commemorates the return of light. ## NT Fulfillment - John 10:22-30: At the Feast of Dedication Jesus declares his unity with the Father. - John 8:12: I am the light of the world. - H…

to.title: Temple Dedication Glory
to.body: # Glory Fills the Temple → Glory Fills the Church ## OT Story — 1 Kings 8:10-13 When the priests bring the ark into the new temple, the cloud fills the house, so the priests cannot stand. The LORD has filled his house with glory. ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:14: The Word became flesh and tabernacled, full of glory. - Acts 2:1-4: The Spirit fills the house at Pentecost. - Revelation 21:22: The Lord G…

from.title: Isaiah 40 Voice Crying
from.body: # Voice in the Wilderness / Prepare the Way → John the Baptist ## OT Story — Isaiah 40:1-11 'Comfort, comfort my people. A voice cries: in the wilderness prepare the way of the LORD. Every valley shall be lifted up.' Glory revealed; all flesh sees together. ## NT Fulfillment - Mark 1:2-3: The opening of the gospel quotes this. - John 1:23: 'I am the voice…' — John's self-identification. - Luke 3:4…

to.title: Isaiah 9 Child Is Born
to.body: # Isaiah 9 / 'For Unto Us a Child Is Born' → Eternal King ## OT Story — Isaiah 9:6-7 'A child is born, a son is given; the government shall be upon his shoulder. His name shall be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.' ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 1:32-33: He will be great and called Son of the Most High; his kingdom no end. - Luke 2:11: Born this day a Savior, C…

from.title: Lamb Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Lamb Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 1:29; Rev 5 Abel's firstborn, Abraham's substitute, the Passover lamb, the daily tamid, Isaiah's silent lamb, John's 'Lamb of God', Revelation's slain Lamb. The lamb is canon's central animal. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 4:4** — Abel's firstborn lamb. - **Gen 22:8** — God will provide himself a lamb. - **Exod 12** — Passover lamb. - **Isa 53:7** — Like a…

to.title: Wine Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Wine Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 2; Matt 26 Noah's vineyard, Melchizedek's wine, Passover cups, Isaiah's wine-feast, Cana's water-into-wine, the cup of the new covenant, the cup of wrath — wine threads the canon. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 9:20** — Noah's vineyard. - **Gen 14:18** — Melchizedek's bread and wine. - **Isa 25:6** — Aged wine on the mountain. - **Jer 25:15** — Cup of wrath…

from.title: Isaiah 50 Third Servant Song
from.body: # Third Servant Song / I Gave My Back → Christ Set His Face ## OT Story — Isaiah 50:4-9 'I gave my back to those who strike, my cheeks to those who pull out the beard… I have set my face like a flint; I know I shall not be put to shame.' ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 9:51: He set his face to go to Jerusalem. - Matthew 26:67: They spat in his face and struck him. - Matthew 27:14: He gave no answer. ## S…

to.title: Wisdom Of Solomon Suffering Righteous
to.body: # Wisdom 2 / The Righteous Mocked → Mockery at the Cross ## OT Story — Wisdom of Solomon 2:12-20 'Let us lie in wait for the righteous man, because he is troublesome to us… He calls himself a child of the Lord. Let us see if his words are true… Let us test him… condemn him to a shameful death.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 27:39-43: 'He trusts in God; let God deliver him… He said, I am the Son of G…

from.title: Lords Prayer Pattern
from.body: # Lord's Prayer Pattern ## NT Text — Matt 6:9-13; Luke 11:2-4 The Lord's Prayer is a compressed covenantal liturgy: Father's name, kingdom, will, bread, forgiveness, deliverance. It echoes Jewish daily prayer while reorienting it around Sonship. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 16:4** — Bread day by day — manna logic behind 'daily bread.' - **Ezek 36:23** — Hallowing God's name in the nations. - **Ps 10…

to.title: Words From Cross Father Forgive
to.body: # Word from Cross: Father, Forgive Them ## NT Text — Luke 23:34 Jesus intercedes for his crucifiers, echoing the Servant who 'made intercession for transgressors.' ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:12** — Intercession for transgressors. - **Num 14:19-20** — Moses intercedes for Israel. - **Gen 50:17** — Joseph forgives brothers. ## Shared structure - Intercession amid suffering - Forgiveness offered - …

from.title: Joseph In Prison With Two
from.body: # Joseph Between Two Prisoners → Christ Between Two Thieves ## OT Story — Genesis 40 In prison Joseph attends two of Pharaoh's officers — cupbearer and baker. He interprets their dreams: one will be lifted up to life, the other lifted up to death. Both happen on the third day. ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 23:32-43: Christ crucified between two criminals — one to life, one to death. - John 6:35,53-58: …

to.title: Joseph Reveals Self
to.body: # Joseph Reveals Himself → Israel Recognizes the Pierced One ## OT Story — Genesis 45:1-15 After testing his brothers, Joseph dismisses his attendants and weeps aloud: 'I am Joseph! Is my father still alive?' The brothers are terrified, then reconciled. He sends them back to bring their father. ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 7:13: Joseph 'made himself known to his brothers' — Stephen uses the verb as ty…

from.title: Bronze Serpent
from.body: # The Bronze Serpent → "The Son of Man Must Be Lifted Up" One of the typologies **Jesus himself names explicitly**. As with Jonah (Mt 12:40), Jesus' own use of this OT story authorizes its typological reading and tells us exactly how he wants it understood. ## OT Story — Numbers 21:4-9 Israel grumbles against God during the wilderness journey. God sends "fiery serpents" whose bite kills many. The …

to.title: Garden Eden Cherubim
to.body: # Eden's Cherubim → Christ as the Way Back ## OT Story — Genesis 3:22-24 After the fall, God says: *"Behold, the man has become like one of us in knowing good and evil. Now, lest he reach out his hand and take also of the tree of life and eat, and live forever..."* (Gen 3:22). He drives the man out, and *"at the east of the garden of Eden he placed the **cherubim** and a **flaming sword** that tur…

from.title: Baptism Of Jesus Theophany
from.body: # Baptism of Jesus as Theophany ## NT Text — Matt 3:13-17; Mark 1:9-11; Luke 3:21-22 Jesus is baptized in the Jordan, the heavens open, the Spirit descends, and the Father speaks. The scene gathers OT theophanies and inaugurates the messianic ministry. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1:2** — Spirit hovering over the waters — new-creation echo. - **Ps 2:7** — You are my Son — coronation formula. - **Isa …

to.title: Flight To Egypt
to.body: # Flight to Egypt ## NT Text — Matt 2:13-15 Joseph takes Mary and the child to Egypt to escape Herod. Israel's history is recapitulated: the Son goes down to Egypt as Israel did, in order to be called out as Israel was. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 46** — Jacob's family goes down to Egypt for refuge. - **Exod 4:19** — Moses told 'all who sought your life are dead' — quoted in Matt 2:20. - **Hos 11:1*…

from.title: Bones Not Broken
from.body: # Bones Not Broken ## NT Text — John 19:33-36 Jesus' legs are not broken; John cites the Passover-lamb statute. The true Passover Lamb dies in conformity to the Exodus rite. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12:46** — Not a bone shall be broken. - **Num 9:12** — Same rule for Passover. - **Ps 34:20** — He keeps all his bones. ## Shared structure - Unbroken bones - Passover statute - Preservation amid dea…

to.title: Revelation 5 Slain Lamb Worthy
to.body: # Revelation 5: Slain Lamb Worthy ## NT Text — Rev 5:6-14 Between the throne and the elders stood a Lamb as though slain. The Lion of Judah is also the slain-yet-standing Lamb. He alone is worthy to open the scroll. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 49:9-10** — Lion of Judah. - **Exod 12** — Passover lamb. - **Isa 53:7** — Lamb led to slaughter. ## Shared structure - Lamb-as-slain - Standing posture - Sev…

from.title: Daniel 6 Lions Den
from.body: # Daniel in the Lions' Den → Sealed Tomb and Vindication ## OT Story — Daniel 6 Daniel prays openly; he is cast to the lions; the stone sealed with the king's signet. At dawn the king finds him alive; God shut the lions' mouths. The accusers are devoured. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 11:33: By faith they stopped the mouths of lions. - 2 Timothy 4:17: I was rescued from the lion's mouth. - Matthew 2…

to.title: Maccabean Faithful Witnesses
to.body: # Maccabean Martyrs → Faithful Unto Death Hoping for Resurrection ## OT Story — 2 Maccabees 7 Seven brothers and their mother are tortured to death by Antiochus for refusing pork. Each affirms resurrection: 'The King of the universe will raise us up.' ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 11:35-38: Women received their dead by resurrection; others were tortured, refusing to accept release, that they might r…

from.title: Crucifixion Between Two Thieves
from.body: # Crucifixion Between Two Thieves ## NT Text — Luke 23:32-43 Jesus is crucified between two criminals, fulfilling Isaiah's 'numbered with the transgressors.' One mocks, one believes — a microcosm of human response. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:12** — Numbered with the transgressors. - **Ps 22:16** — Pierce my hands and feet. - **Zech 13:7** — Strike the shepherd. ## Shared structure - Three crosse…

to.title: Samson Solo Victory In Death
to.body: # Samson's Death Brings Greater Victory → Christ Triumphs by Dying ## OT Story — Judges 16:23-31 Blinded, mocked, between two pillars, Samson prays once more. He pushes; the temple collapses; he kills more in his death than in his life. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 2:14-15: Through death he destroyed the one who has the power of death. - Colossians 2:15: He disarmed the powers, triumphing over them…

from.title: Hosea Out Of Egypt Called My Son
from.body: # Hosea 11 / 'Out of Egypt I Called My Son' → Christ Recapitulates Israel ## OT Story — Hosea 11:1 'When Israel was a child, I loved him, and out of Egypt I called my son.' A recall of the exodus. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 2:15: Quoted at the holy family's return from Egypt. - Exodus 4:22: Israel is my firstborn son. - Romans 9:25-26: Hosea quoted again for Gentile inclusion. ## Shared structure…

to.title: Voice From Heaven Beloved Son
to.body: # Voice from Heaven: Beloved Son ## NT Text — Matt 3:17; 17:5 At baptism and transfiguration the Father declares, 'This is my beloved Son.' The formula fuses Psalm 2's royal coronation with Isaiah 42's Servant and possibly Genesis 22's 'only/beloved son.' ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 2:7** — Royal son begotten today. - **Isa 42:1** — Chosen in whom my soul delights. - **Gen 22:2** — Take your son, you…

from.title: Colossians 1 Cosmic Christ
from.body: # Colossians 1: Cosmic Christ ## NT Text — Col 1:15-20 Christ is the image of God, firstborn over creation; in him all things hold together. The hymn weaves Genesis, Proverbs 8 wisdom, and reconciliation. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1** — Creator of all things. - **Prov 8:22-31** — Wisdom present at creation. - **Ps 89:27** — Firstborn, highest of kings. ## Shared structure - Image of God - Pre-exis…

to.title: Colossians 2 Buried And Raised With Christ
to.body: # Colossians 2: Buried and Raised with Christ ## NT Text — Col 2:11-13 Believers are circumcised with a circumcision made without hands, buried with Christ in baptism, raised with him through faith. Old self stripped, new self received. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 30:6** — Circumcise your heart. - **Jer 4:4** — Circumcise yourselves to the LORD. - **Ezek 36:26** — New heart and new spirit. ## Share…

from.title: Pierced Side Blood And Water
from.body: # Pierced Side: Blood and Water ## NT Text — John 19:34-37 A soldier pierces Jesus' side; blood and water flow. John cites Zechariah 12:10. ## OT Antecedents - **Zech 12:10** — They will look on him whom they pierced. - **Exod 17:6** — Water from struck rock. - **Ezek 47:1** — Water from temple. ## Shared structure - Pierced side - Blood and water - Look-and-mourn - Temple-water echo ## Theologica…

to.title: Zechariah 13 Strike The Shepherd
to.body: # Strike the Shepherd → Sheep Scattered at Gethsemane ## OT Story — Zechariah 13:7 'Awake, O sword, against my shepherd, against the man who stands next to me… Strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 26:31: 'I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.' - Mark 14:27: Same citation at Gethsemane. - John 16:32: You will be scattered to your …

from.title: Joseph Sold By Brothers
from.body: # Joseph Sold by His Brothers → The Betrayal and Vindication of Christ ## OT Story — Genesis 37, 40, 41-50 Joseph is the **beloved son** of Jacob, given a special garment (the "coat of many colors"). He has prophetic dreams in which his brothers and parents bow down to him. His brothers hate him and plot to kill him; instead, on Judah's suggestion, they sell him for **twenty pieces of silver** to …

to.title: Esther Intercessor
to.body: # Esther → Christ as Intercessor ## OT Story — Esther 4-8 Haman, the king's vizier, has secured a decree to annihilate the Jews throughout the Persian empire (Esth 3:13). Mordecai sends word to Esther — now queen — asking her to plead for her people. The problem: anyone who approaches the king unsummoned faces death *"unless the king holds out the golden scepter"* (Esth 4:11). Mordecai's challenge…

from.title: Darkness Over Land
from.body: # Darkness Over the Land ## NT Text — Matt 27:45 Darkness covers the land from noon to three. The cosmic sign echoes the ninth Egyptian plague and Amos's day-of-the-Lord. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 10:21-23** — Three days darkness over Egypt. - **Amos 8:9** — Sun goes down at noon. - **Joel 2:31** — Sun turned to darkness. ## Shared structure - Daylight extinguished - Cosmic mourning - Plague echo…

to.title: Road To Emmaus
to.body: # Road to Emmaus ## NT Text — Luke 24:13-32 The risen Christ walks with two disciples and opens 'Moses and all the prophets' to show the necessity of messianic suffering. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 18:15** — Prophet like Moses. - **Isa 53** — Suffering Servant. - **Ps 22; Ps 16** — Suffering and resurrection psalms. ## Shared structure - Walking journey - Stranger reveals Scripture - Hearts burnin…

from.title: Seed Of Abraham
from.body: # Seed of Abraham ## The oracle In Abraham's seed all the families of the earth shall be blessed; the seed will be as numerous as the stars and will possess the gate of his enemies. ## Date / context Promised to Abraham in Ur, Canaan, and on Mount Moriah after the Aqedah, c. 2000 BC. ## Fulfillment Paul reads "seed" as singular, fulfilled in Christ (Galatians 3:16), through whom blessing flows to …

to.title: Shiloh Of Judah
to.body: # Shiloh of Judah ## The oracle Jacob, blessing his sons, declares that the scepter shall not depart from Judah, nor a ruler's staff from between his feet, until Shiloh come, and to him shall be the obedience of the peoples. ## Date / context Patriarchal blessing in Egypt at the end of Genesis, c. 1800 BC. ## Fulfillment Fulfilled in Christ, the Lion of the tribe of Judah (Revelation 5:5), who is …

from.title: Burning Bush I AM
from.body: # Burning Bush / I AM → Christ as I AM ## OT Story — Exodus 3:1-15 Moses sees a bush burning yet not consumed. God speaks: 'I AM WHO I AM.' This is His name forever, His memorial-name from generation to generation. ## NT Fulfillment - John 8:58: 'Before Abraham was, I AM' — Jesus identifies with the divine name. - John 18:5-6: 'I am he' and the cohort falls backward. - Acts 7:30-34: Stephen recoun…

to.title: Daniel 3 Fourth Man
to.body: # Three in the Furnace / Fourth Man → Christ With His People in the Fire ## OT Story — Daniel 3 Shadrach, Meshach, Abednego refuse the idol; Nebuchadnezzar throws them into a furnace seven times hotter. He sees four men walking — the fourth like a son of the gods. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 8:31-39: Nothing can separate us from God's love — neither tribulation. - Isaiah 43:2: When you walk through…
